blocks:
Surah Al-Hajj

It is Meccan, according to Ibn Abbas and Ata, except for some verses. Al-Hasan said they are six verses. And some said they are other than four verses. The number of its verses is seventy-eight verses (Kufi count), seventy-seven (Makki count), seventy-six (Madani count), seventy-five (Basri count), seventy-four (Shami count).

بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ

(In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اتَّقُوا رَبَّكُمْ إِنَّ زَلْزَلَةَ السَّاعَةِ شَيْءٌ عَظِيمٌ (1) O mankind! Be wary of your Lord! Indeed the earthquake of the Hour is a terrible thing.

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "O assembly of people! Piety (التَّقوى), piety (التَّقوى)! Beware of the Hour, just as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has said: إِنَّ زَلْزَلَةَ السَّاعَةِ شَيْءٌ عَظِيمٌ (Indeed the earthquake of the Hour is a terrible thing)."

And Al-Qummi said: It is an address to mankind generally. It was said that it is an earthquake that occurs before the sun rises from its setting place (مغربها), and it is one of the signs (أشراط) of the Hour.

(٢) يَوْمَ تَرَوْنَها تَذْهَلُ كُلُّ مُرْضِعَةٍ عَمَّا أَرْضَعَتْ ** (2) On the day you see it, every suckling mother will forget her suckling It is said that this is a depiction of its terror, and the pronoun (الضّمير) refers to the earthquake (الزلزلة). The intention is to indicate that its terror is such that when the one who has placed her breast in the infant's mouth is stunned, she will pull it away from his mouth and forget him. وَتَضَعُ كُلُّ ذاتِ حَمْلٍ حَمْلَها ** and every pregnant female will abort her fetus, Her fetus (جنينها). Al-Qummi said: Every woman who dies pregnant at the time of the earthquake of the Hour will abort her fetus on the Day of Resurrection. وَتَرَى النَّاسَ سُكارى ** and you will see the people drunk, As if they are drunk, وَما هُمْ بِسُكارى ** though they will not be drunk, in reality. And it was recited (قُرِئَ) as (سُكْرَى) in both instances. وَلَكِنَّ عَذابَ اللهِ شَدِيدٌ. but Allah’s punishment is severe. Al-Qummi said: It means their minds are gone due to grief and terror, bewildered in the gathering (المجمع). Imran ibn Husayn and Abu Sa'id al-Khudri said: The two verses from the beginning of the Surah were revealed at night during the campaign (غزاة) of Banu al-Mustaliq, who are a tribe (حيّ) from Khuzā‘ah, while the people were traveling. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم called out, and the mounts knelt down until they were gathered around the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He recited them to them, and no one saw more weeping than on that night. When they awoke, they did not remove the saddles (السّرج) from the beasts nor did they pitch the tents, and the people were between one weeping and one sitting sorrowful and contemplating. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "Do you know which day that is?" They said: "Allah and His Messenger know best." He said: "That is the Day when Allah the Almighty says to Adam: 'Send forth the contingent of the Fire (بعث النار) from your offspring.' Adam will say: 'From how many? How many?' So He, the Mighty and Majestic, will say: 'From every thousand, nine hundred and ninety-nine to the Fire, and one to Paradise.' This was grievous (كبر) to the Muslims, and they wept. They said: 'Then who will be saved, O Messenger of Allah?'" He said: "Receive good tidings (أبشروا), for with you are two creations (خليقتين), Gog and Magog (يأجوج ومأجوج). They were never in anything except that they increased its number. You are among the people only like a white hair on a black bull, or like a mark (رقم) (1) on the forearm of a young camel (البكر), or like a mole (شامة) on the side of a camel (البعير)." Then he said: "I hope that you will be a quarter of the people of Paradise." So they exclaimed: Allahu Akbar (فكبروا). Then he said: "I hope that you will be a third of the people of Paradise." So they exclaimed: Allahu Akbar (فكبروا). Then he said: "I hope that you will be two-thirds of the people of Paradise, for the people of Paradise are one hundred and twenty rows, eighty of which are my Ummah." Then he said: "And seventy thousand from my Ummah will enter Paradise without reckoning." And in some narrations (الروايات): Umar ibn al-Khattab said: "O Messenger of Allah, seventy thousand?" He said: "Yes, and with every one of them, seventy thousand." Then Ukkasha ibn Mihsan stood up and said: "O Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, supplicate to Allah to make me one of them." He said: "O Allah, make him one of them." Then a man from the Ansar stood up and said: "Supplicate to Allah to make me one of them." He said: "Ukkasha has preceded you in this." Ibn Abbas said: The Ansari was a hypocrite (منافقاً), and that is why He did not supplicate for him.

(٣) وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ يُجادِلُ فِي اللهِ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ** (3) And among the people is he who disputes concerning Allah without any knowledge, He contends (يُخَاصِمُ). وَيَتَّبِعُ كُلَّ شَيْطانٍ مَرِيدٍ ** and follows every rebellious devil. Stripped bare (مُتَجَرِّد) for corruption (لِلْفَسَاد), and the nakedness (الْعُرِيّ) has led him astray (أَضَلَّهُ). And al-Qummi said: al-Marid (الْمَرِيد) is the wicked one (الْخَبِيث). It was said that it was revealed concerning al-Nadr ibn al-Harith, who was argumentative (جَدِلًا). He used to say that the angels are the daughters of Allah, and that the Quran is tales of the ancients (أَسَاطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ), and that there is no resurrection after death. And [the verse] encompasses him and those like him (أَضْرَابَهُ).

(٤) كُتِبَ عَلَيْهِ ** (It has been decreed for him) upon the Devil أَنَّهُ مَنْ تَوَلَّاهُ ** (that whoever takes him as a guardian) (follows him) فَأَنَّهُ يُضِلُّهُ (then he will mislead him). Meaning, the misleading (إِضْلَال) of whoever takes him as a guardian has been decreed for him, because he was created upon it (جُبِلَ عَلَيْهِ). **وَيَهْدِيهِ إِلى عَذابِ السَّعِيرِ ** (and guides him to the punishment of the Blaze) by inducing him toward that which leads to it.

(٥) يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ فِي رَيْبٍ مِنَ الْبَعْثِ (O mankind! If you are in doubt about the Resurrection,) Regarding its possibility and its being within [Allah's] power. فَإِنَّا خَلَقْناكُمْ (then [consider that] We created you)—meaning: look at the beginning of your creation, for that will dispel your doubt—مِنْ تُرابٍ (from dust), through the creation of Adam from it, and through the creation of the nutrients from which the semen is formed. ثُمَّ مِنْ نُطْفَةٍ (then from a drop of semen,) Semen (مَنِيّ) from the drop (النُّطْف), which is the pouring (الصَّبّ). ثُمَّ مِنْ عَلَقَةٍ (then from a clot of blood,) A piece of congealed (جَامِدَة) blood. ثُمَّ مِنْ مُضْغَةٍ (then from a lump of flesh,) A piece of flesh, which originally is the amount that is chewed (يُمْضَغ).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The drop of semen (النُّطْفَة) is white, like thick phlegm (النُّخَامَة). It remains in the womb, once it enters it, for forty days. Then it turns into a clot (عَلَقَة). He said: It is a clot like the congealed blood clot of a cupping glass (المِحْجَمَة). It remains in the womb after its transformation from the drop of semen for forty days. Then it turns into a lump of flesh (مُضْغَة). He said: It is a red lump of flesh (مُضْغَة) in which there are intertwined green veins (عُرُوق خُضْر مُشْتَبِكَة). Then it turns into bone, and hearing and sight are opened for it, and its limbs are arranged. مُخَلَّقَةٍ وَغَيْرِ مُخَلَّقَةٍ (formed and unformed,) Al-Qummi said: The formed (المُخَلَّقَة) is when it becomes complete (تَامّاً), and the unformed (غَيْرِ مُخَلَّقَةٍ) is the miscarriage (السَّقْط). لِنُبَيِّنَ لَكُمْ (so that We may clarify for you.) It is said that the omission of the object (المَفْعُول) is an allusion to the fact that these actions of His clarify aspects of His power and wisdom that cannot be encompassed by mention (الذِّكْر).

And Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): لِنُبَيِّنَ لَكُمْ (so that We may clarify for you) that you were like that in the wombs. وَنُقِرُّ فِي الْأَرْحامِ ما نَشاءُ (and We settle in the wombs whatever We wish) so that it does not exit as a miscarriage (سَقْطاً).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about that, and he said: The formed (المُخَلَّقَة) are the particles (الذَّرّ) whom Allah created in the loins of Adam, from whom He took the covenant (المِيثَاق). Then He caused them to flow through the loins of men and the wombs of women, and they are those who emerge into the world until they are questioned about the covenant (المِيثَاق). As for His saying وَغَيْرِ مُخَلَّقَةٍ (and unformed), they are every soul (نَسَمَة) whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, did not create in the loins of Adam when He created the particles (الذَّرّ) and took the covenant from them. They are the drops of semen (النُّطَف) resulting from al-ʿazl (العَزْل) [coitus interruptus] and the miscarriage (السَّقْط) before the spirit (الرُّوح), life (الحَيَاة), and permanence (البَقَاء) are breathed into it.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The drop of semen (النُّطْفَة) remains in the womb for forty days, then it becomes a clot (عَلَقَة) for forty days, then it becomes a lump of flesh (مُضْغَة) for forty days. When four months are completed, Allah sends two creating angels (مَلَكَيْن خَلَّاقَيْن). They say: "O Lord, what shall we create? Male or female?" They are commanded. They say: "O Lord, wretched (شَقِيّاً) or blessed (سَعِيداً)?" They are commanded. They say: "O Lord, what is His term (أَجَلُهُ), and what is His provision (رِزْقُهُ), and everything concerning His state?" And He enumerated things from that, and they write the covenant (المِيثَاق) between His eyes. When Allah completes the term (الأَجَل), Allah sends an angel who gives it a shout (زَجْرَة), and it emerges having forgotten the covenant (المِيثَاق). إِلى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى (until a designated term,) Which is the time of delivery (الوَضْع). Its minimum is six months, and its maximum is nine.

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: A woman does not give birth in less than six months.

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the maximum duration of pregnancy for a child in his mother's womb, as people say that it sometimes remains in her womb for years. He said: "They lie. The maximum limit of pregnancy is nine months; it does not increase by a moment. If it were to increase by an hour, it would kill its mother before it emerges."

And narrated from al-Sadiq and al-Kazim (peace be upon them both): If she gives birth to it after more than a year, she is not to be believed, not even for a single hour. ثُمَّ يُخْرِجُكُمْ طِفْلاً ثُمَّ لِتَبْلُغُوا أَشُدَّكُمْ (then He brings you forth as infants, then [He sustains you] so that you may reach your maturity.) Your perfection (كَمَالُكُمْ) in strength (القُوَّة) and intellect (العَقْل).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The cessation of the orphan's (اليَتِيم) orphanhood (يُتْم) is puberty (الاحْتِلَام), and that is his maturity (أَشُدُّهُ). وَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُتَوَفَّى (and among you are those who are taken away [by death]) Upon reaching maturity (الأَشُدّ) or before it. وَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُرَدُّ إِلى أَرْذَلِ الْعُمُرِ (and among you are those who are relegated to the most abject age,) Senility (الهَرَم) and dotage (الخَرَف).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father (peace be upon them both), he said: When the servant reaches one hundred years, that is the most abject age (أَرْذَلُ الْعُمُر).

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Seventy-five, as preceded in Surat al-Nahl. لِكَيْلا يَعْلَمَ مِنْ بَعْدِ عِلْمٍ شَيْئاً (so that he knows nothing after [having had] knowledge.) So that he returns to his state during the time of childhood (الطُّفُولِيَّة) in terms of feebleness of intellect (سَخَافَة العَقْل) and lack of understanding (قِلَّة الفَهْم), thus forgetting what he did and denying what he knew. وَتَرَى الْأَرْضَ هامِدَةً (And you see the earth lifeless,) Dead (مَيْتَة) and dry (يَابِسَة). فَإِذا أَنْزَلْنا عَلَيْهَا الْماءَ اهْتَزَّتْ (but when We send down water upon it, it stirs,) It moves (تَحَرَّكَتْ) with vegetation. وَرَبَتْ (and swells,) And expands (انْتَفَخَتْ). وَأَنْبَتَتْ مِنْ كُلِّ زَوْجٍ (and brings forth every splendid kind [of plant].) Kind (صِنْف). بَهِيجٍ (splendid.) Beautiful (حَسَن) and delightful (رَائِق).

(٦) ذلِكَ ** What was mentioned concerning the creation of man in different stages, his transformation through contradictory states, and the revival of the earth after its death, بِأَنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الْحَقُ ** (is) because Allah is the Reality, that He is the Firm in His essence (ذاته), by Whom things are realized (يتحقّق). وَأَنَّهُ يُحْيِ الْمَوْتى ** and that He revives the dead, and that He is capable of reviving them. Otherwise, He would not have revived the sperm-drop (النّطفة) and the dead earth. وَأَنَّهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ ** and that He has power over all things. because His power belongs to His essence (ذاته), whose relation to everything is equal.

(7) وَأَنَّ السَّاعَةَ آتِيَةٌ لا رَيْبَ فِيها ** (7) and that the Hour is bound to come, there is no doubt in it, For change is a proof of cessation (الانصرام) and renewal (التجدد). وَأَنَ اللهَ يَبْعَثُ مَنْ فِي الْقُبُورِ ** and that Allah will raise those who are in the graves. in accordance with His promise.

In Qurb al-Isnad, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny) said to Gabriel: "O Gabriel, show me how Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, will resurrect the servants on the Day of Resurrection." He said: "Yes." So they went out to the graveyard of Banu Sa'idah. He approached a grave and said to him: "Come out by the permission of Allah." A man came out, shaking the dust from his head, saying: "Woe is my regret (وا لهفاه)! And regret (اللهف) is destruction (الثبور)!" Then he said: "Enter," and he entered. Then he directed him to another grave and said: "Come out by the permission of Allah." A young man came out, shaking the dust from his head, saying: "I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, He is One, He has no partner, and I bear witness that Muhammad is His servant and His Messenger, and I bear witness **أَنَّ السَّاعَةَ آتِيَةٌ لا رَيْبَ فِيها وَأَنَّ اللهَ يَبْعَثُ مَنْ فِي الْقُبُورِ ** that the Hour is bound to come, there is no doubt in it, and that Allah will raise those who are in the graves. Then he said: "Thus will they be resurrected on the Day of Resurrection."

And al-Qummi narrates what is close to this, and it will come in Surat al-Zumar.

And in al-Majalis and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: When Allah wills to resurrect the creation, He will send rain from the sky upon the earth for forty mornings, so the joints (الأوصال) will gather and the flesh (اللّحوم) will grow.

(8) وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ يُجادِلُ فِي اللهِ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ وَلا هُدىً وَلا كِتابٍ مُنِيرٍ (8) And among the people is he who disputes concerning Allah without any knowledge or guidance or an illuminating Book. (9) ثانِيَ عِطْفِهِ ** being arrogant, for turning the side away (ثنى العطف) is a metaphor (كناية) for arrogance, [meaning] he turned his neck/side away, لِيُضِلَّ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ. And it was recited with a fathah on the yā’ [in لِيُضِلَّ]. لَهُ فِي الدُّنْيا خِزْيٌ وَنُذِيقُهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ عَذابَ الْحَرِيقِ** (9) turning his side away, so that he may lead [people] astray from the way of Allah. For him is disgrace in this world, and on the Day of Resurrection We shall make him taste the punishment of the burning.

Al-Qummi said: This Ayah was revealed concerning Abu Jahl. Regarding ثانِيَ عِطْفِهِ , he said: He turned away from the truth. Regarding عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ , he said: From the path (طريق) of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), and faith (الإيمان).

In Misbah al-Shari'ah, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever disputes with creation concerning that which he is not commanded regarding, then he has contended with the quality of being the Creator (الخالقية) and the quality of being the Lord (الربوبية)." Allah the Almighty said: وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ يُجادِلُ ** [until the end of] the Ayah. And He said: "There is no one whose punishment is more severe than he who wears the garment of devotion (النسك) based on mere claim (الدعوى), without reality (حقيقة) or meaning (معنى)."

(١٠) ذلِكَ بِما قَدَّمَتْ يَداكَ وَأَنَّ اللهَ لَيْسَ بِظَلَّامٍ لِلْعَبِيدِ (10) That is because of what your hands have sent ahead, and because Allah is not tyrannical to the servants. (١١) وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ يَعْبُدُ اللهَ عَلى حَرْفٍ ** on the edge (على طرف) of the religion, having no firmness (لا ثبات) in it, like one who is on the edge (على طرف) of the army; for if he perceives victory (ظفر), he remains, and if not, he flees. فَإِنْ أَصابَهُ خَيْرٌ اطْمَأَنَ بِهِ وَإِنْ أَصابَتْهُ فِتْنَةٌ انْقَلَبَ عَلى وَجْهِهِ خَسِرَ الدُّنْيا وَالْآخِرَةَ ** by the loss of his protection (عصمته) and the nullification (حبوط) of his deeds due to apostasy (الارتداد). **ذلِكَ هُوَ الْخُسْرانُ الْمُبِينُ ** since there is no loss like it. (11) And among the people is he who worships Allah on a precarious footing. If a good fortune befalls him, he is content with it, but if an ordeal befalls him, he turns about on his face: he loses this world and the Hereafter. That is the manifest loss.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah. He said: "They are a people who affirmed the Oneness of Allah (وحّدوا الله) and abandoned the worship of those worshipped besides Allah, thus exiting polytheism (الشّرك). However, they did not recognize that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). So they worship Allah while being in doubt (شكّ) concerning Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and what He brought. They came to the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and said: 'We shall observe. If our wealth increases and we are granted well-being (عُوفينا) in ourselves and our children, we will know that He is truthful and that He is the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). But if it is otherwise, we shall reconsider (نظرنا).' The Almighty Allah said: فَإِنْ أَصابَهُ خَيْرٌ اطْمَأَنَّ بِهِ ** meaning well-being (عافية) in this world. وَإِنْ أَصابَتْهُ فِتْنَةٌ meaning affliction (بلاء) upon himself. انْقَلَبَ عَلى وَجْهِهِ He turned back upon his doubt (شكّه) towards polytheism (الشرك)."

(١٢) يَدْعُوا مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ما لا يَضُرُّهُ وَما لا يَنْفَعُهُ (He calls, instead of Allah, on that which neither harms him nor benefits him.) He (peace be upon him) said: He turns back as a polytheist (مشركاً), calling upon other than Allah and worshipping other than Him. So, among them is he who recognizes [the truth], and faith enters his heart, so he believes and affirms, and he departs from his station of doubt (الشكّ) to faith (الايمان). And among them is he who remains firm upon his doubt (شكّه), and among them is he who turns back to polytheism (الشرك). And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The like of it, without my interpretation of al-khayr (الخير) and al-fitnah (الفتنة). ذلِكَ هُوَ الضَّلالُ الْبَعِيدُ (That is the far error) from the goal (المقصد).

(١٣) يَدْعُوا لَمَنْ ضَرُّهُ (He calls upon one whose harm) by virtue of its being worshipped (بكونه معبوداً), because it necessitates destruction (القتل) in this world (في الدّنيا) and punishment (العذاب) in the Hereafter (في الآخرة). أَقْرَبُ مِنْ نَفْعِهِ ** (is nearer than his benefit) which is expected through its worship (بعبادته), and that is intercession (الشفاعة) and seeking nearness (التوسّل) through it to Allah. لَبِئْسَ الْمَوْلى ** (Certainly, evil is the patron) (meaning) the helper (الناصر). وَلَبِئْسَ الْعَشِيرُ (and evil is the companion.) (meaning) the associate (الصاحب).

(The full verse is: يَدْعُوا لَمَنْ ضَرُّهُ أَقْرَبُ مِنْ نَفْعِهِ لَبِئْسَ الْمَوْلى وَلَبِئْسَ الْعَشِيرُ) (13) He calls upon one whose harm is nearer than his benefit. Certainly, evil is the patron, and evil is the companion.

(١٤) **إِنَّ اللهَ يُدْخِلُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ إِنَّ اللهَ يَفْعَلُ ما يُرِيدُ ** (14) Indeed Allah will admit those who have faith and do righteous deeds into gardens with streams running in them. Indeed Allah does whatever He desires. This includes His rewarding of the righteous monotheist and His punishing of the polytheist, for which there is no repeller nor preventer.

(١٥) مَنْ كانَ يَظُنُّ أَنْ لَنْ يَنْصُرَهُ اللهُ فِي الدُّنْيا وَالْآخِرَةِ فَلْيَمْدُدْ بِسَبَبٍ إِلَى السَّماءِ ثُمَّ لْيَقْطَعْ (15) Whoever thinks that Allah will not help him in this world and the Hereafter, let him stretch a rope to the ceiling, and then let him cut it off.

And it was read with the kasrah on the lām [i.e., لِيَقْطَعْ], فَلْيَنْظُرْ هَلْ يُذْهِبَنَّ كَيْدُهُ ما يَغِيظُ (let him see if his stratagem will remove what enrages him).

It was said that its meaning is that Allah is the Helper of His Messenger (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) in this world and the Hereafter. So, whoever thinks otherwise and anticipates it out of his rage (غيظه) or despair (جزعه), let him exert himself fully in removing his rage or despair by doing what one who is filled with anger or excessive despair does: namely, stretching a rope to the ceiling (سماء بيته) of his house and strangling himself by cutting [the breath] when he strangles himself, for the one who strangles himself cuts off his own breath by blocking its passages. Or, let him stretch a rope to the sky (سماء الدنيا) of the world, and then let him cut the distance with it until he reaches its limit (عنانه), striving to repel His help (نصره). And it was said that the meaning intended by help (النصر) is provision (الرزق), and the pronoun refers to the one [who thinks].

And al-Qummi [said]: The word al-ẓann (الظنّ) in the Book of Allah is used in two ways: the ẓann of certainty (ظنّ يقين) and the ẓann of doubt (ظنّ شكّ). This [instance] is the ẓann of doubt. He said: Whoever doubts that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will not help His Messenger (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) فِي الدُّنْيا وَالْآخِرَةِ فَلْيَمْدُدْ بِسَبَبٍ إِلَى السَّماءِ (in this world and the Hereafter, let him stretch a rope to the ceiling), meaning: let him establish a proof (دليلاً) between himself and Allah. And the Almighty Allah said: ثُمَّ لْيَقْطَعْ (and then let him cut it off), meaning: let him distinguish (يميّز). And the proof that al-sabab (السبب) is the proof (الدليل) is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in Surat al-Kahf: وَآتَيْناهُ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ سَبَباً فَأَتْبَعَ سَبَباً (and We gave him a way to everything, and he followed a way), meaning: a proof (دليلاً). And He said: ثُمَّ لْيَقْطَعْ (and then let him cut it off), meaning: let him distinguish (يميّز). And the proof that al-qaṭʿ (القطع) is distinguishing (التميز) is His saying, the Almighty: وَقَطَّعْناهُمُ اثْنَتَيْ عَشْرَةَ أَسْباطاً (and We divided them into twelve tribes), meaning: nations (امماً), i.e., We distinguished them (ميّزناهم). فَلْيَنْظُرْ هَلْ يُذْهِبَنَّ كَيْدُهُ ما يَغِيظُ (let him see if his stratagem will remove what enrages him), meaning: his trick (حيلته). And the proof that al-kayd (الكيد) is the trick (الحيلة) is His saying, the Almighty: وَكَذلِكَ كِدْنا لِيُوسُفَ (Thus did We devise for Joseph), meaning: We tricked (احتلنا) for him until he detained his brother. And His saying, relating the saying of Pharaoh: فَأَجْمِعُوا كَيْدَكُمْ (So muster your stratagem), meaning: your trick (حيلتكم). He said: So, when he establishes a proof (سبباً) for himself and distinguishes (وَمَيَّزَ), it guides him (دَلَّهُ) to the truth (الحقّ).

As for the general public (العامّة), they narrated concerning this: that whoever does not believe what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has said, let him throw a rope to the ceiling of the house and then let him strangle himself.

(16) وَكَذلِكَ أَنْزَلْناهُ We sent down the entire Qur'an آياتٍ بَيِّناتٍ clear (واضحات) وَأَنَّ اللهَ يَهْدِي by means of it (به) مَنْ يُرِيدُ (16) Thus We have sent it down as manifest signs, and Allah guides whomever He wills. (17) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَالَّذِينَ هادُوا وَالصَّابِئِينَ وَالنَّصارى وَالْمَجُوسَ وَالَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا إِنَّ اللهَ يَفْصِلُ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ by judging (بالحكومة) between them, and by making manifest the one among them who is truthful (المحق) from the one who is false (المبطل), and by recompensing (وجزاء) each one with what is fitting for him (ما يليق به). إِنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيدٌ Knower (عالم) of it (به), watchful (مراقب) over its states (لأحواله). (17) Indeed, Allah will separate the faithful, the Jews, the Sabaeans, the Christians, the Magians and the polytheists on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed Allah is witness to all things.

(١٨) أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللهَ يَسْجُدُ لَهُ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** submits to His command وَالشَّمْسُ وَالْقَمَرُ وَالنُّجُومُ وَالْجِبالُ وَالشَّجَرُ وَالدَّوَابُّ وَكَثِيرٌ مِنَ النَّاسِ ** (18) Have you not regarded that Allah is He Whom prostrates to whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth, and the sun, and the moon, and the stars, and the mountains, and the trees, and the animals, and many of mankind. A discussion concerning the explanation of this prostration will come in Surat al-Nur, if Allah wills. وَكَثِيرٌ حَقَّ عَلَيْهِ الْعَذابُ ** due to his disbelief and his refusal (إبائه) of obedience and submission (الانقياد). وَمَنْ يُهِنِ اللهُ فَما لَهُ مِنْ مُكْرِمٍ إِنَّ اللهَ يَفْعَلُ ما يَشاءُ.** And many are those against whom the punishment is due, and whomever Allah humiliates, there will be none to honor him. Indeed Allah does whatever He wishes.

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them): It was said to him that a man was speaking about the Will (المشيّة). So he said: "Call him to me." He said: So he was called to him. He said to him: "O servant of Allah, did Allah create you for what He willed, or for what you willed?" He replied: "For what He willed." He said: "Does He make you ill when He wills, or when you will?" He replied: "When He wills." He said: "Does He cure you when He wills, or when you will?" He replied: "When He wills." He said: "Does He admit you where He wills, or where you will?" He replied: "Where He wills." He said: Then Ali (peace be upon him) said: "Had you said otherwise, I would have struck that which contains your eyes."

(١٩) هذانِ خَصْمانِ (19) These two are adversaries Two contending groups: the believers and the disbelievers. اخْتَصَمُوا فِي رَبِّهِمْ (who contend concerning their Lord) Al-Qummi said: We and Banu Umayyah. We said Allah and His Messenger spoke the truth, and Banu Umayyah said Allah and His Messenger lied. And in al-Khisal, narrated from al-Husayn (عليه السلام): similar to it, and He added: "So we are the two contending parties (الخصمان) on the Day of Resurrection." فَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (As for those who disbelieved) A judgment for their contention. It is said this is the meaning of His saying, the Almighty: إِنَّ اللهَ يَفْصِلُ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ (Indeed, Allah will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection.) Al-Qummi: فَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (As for those who disbelieved) means Banu Umayyah. **قُطِّعَتْ لَهُمْ ثِيابٌ مِنْ نارٍ يُصَبُّ مِنْ فَوْقِ رُؤُسِهِمُ الْحَمِيمُ ** (garments of fire will be cut out for them, and boiling water will be poured over their heads.) The hot water.

(٢٠) يُصْهَرُ بِهِ ما فِي بُطُونِهِمْ وَالْجُلُودُ ** (20) whereby what is in their bellies and their skins will be melted. meaning, due to the intensity of its heat, it affects their insides just as it affects their outsides, so their entrails are melted by it, just as their skins are melted by it. (٢١) وَلَهُمْ مَقامِعُ ** (21) And for them are maces whips (سياط) **مِنْ حَدِيدٍ ** (of iron) with which they are lashed.

Al-Qummi said: The Fire disfigures him, so his lower lip slackens until it reaches his navel, and his upper lip shrinks until it reaches the middle of his head. وَلَهُمْ مَقامِعُ مِنْ حَدِيدٍ ** (And for them are maces of iron). He said: These are the pillars (الأعمدة) with which they are struck.

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, He said: وَلَهُمْ مَقامِعُ مِنْ حَدِيدٍ ** (And for them are maces of iron). If one iron mace (مقمع) were placed on the earth, and then the two weighty ones (الثقلان) gathered over it, they would not be able to lift it from the earth.

(٢٢) كُلَّما أَرادُوا أَنْ يَخْرُجُوا مِنْها مِنْ غَمٍّ أُعِيدُوا فِيها ** (22) Whenever they desire to leave it because of anguish, they will be returned to it (They are struck) by those pillars (of iron). And it is said to them: ذُوقُوا عَذابَ الْحَرِيقِ ** "Taste the punishment of the burning fire," which is the Fire that reaches the extreme in burning (الإحراق).

In al-Qummi, narrated from Abu Basir, from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). He said: I said to him: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, frighten me, for my heart has become hard." He (peace be upon him) said: "O Abu Muhammad, prepare yourself for the long life. Indeed, Gabriel came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم while He was frowning (قاطب), though before that He used to come smiling. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: 'O Gabriel, you have come to me frowning today.' He replied: 'O Muhammad, the bellows (منافخ) of the Fire have been set up.' He (the Prophet) asked: 'And what are the bellows (منافخ) of the Fire, O Gabriel?' He replied: 'O Muhammad, indeed Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded the Fire, and it was blown upon for a thousand years until it became white. Then it was blown upon for a thousand years until it became red. Then it was blown upon for a thousand years until it became black. So, it is black and dark. If a single drop of al-Dari' (الضّريع) were to fall into the drink of the people of the world, its inhabitants would die from its stench. And if a single ring of the chain, whose length is seventy cubits (ذراعاً), were placed upon the world, the world would melt from its heat. And if a garment (سربال) from the garments (سرابيل) of the people of the Fire were hung between the heaven and the earth, the people of the earth would die from its smell and its blaze (وهجه).' He (the narrator) said: Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم wept, and Gabriel wept. So Allah sent an angel to them, who said to them: 'Indeed, your Lord conveys His greetings (السلام) to you and says: I have secured you both from committing a sin for which I would punish you.' Abu Abdillah (Imam al-Sadiq) said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was never seen smiling after that. Then he (Imam al-Sadiq) said: 'Indeed, the people of the Fire magnify the Fire, and the people of Paradise magnify Paradise and the bliss (النعيم). And when they enter Hell (جهنّم), they fall (هَوَوْا) into it for a distance of seventy years. When they reach its highest point, they are struck down with iron maces (مقامع الحديد) and returned to its lowest depth (دركها). This is their state, and it is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **كُلَّما أَرادُوا أَنْ يَخْرُجُوا مِنْها مِنْ غَمٍّ أُعِيدُوا فِيها وَذُوقُوا عَذابَ الْحَرِيقِ ** (Whenever they desire to leave it because of anguish, they will be returned to it, and [it will be said,] 'Taste the punishment of the burning fire'). Then their skins are replaced with skins other than those they had.' Abu Abdillah (Imam al-Sadiq) said: 'Is that enough for you, O Abu Muhammad?' I said: 'It is enough for me, it is enough for me.'"

(23) إِنَّ اللهَ يُدْخِلُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ ** (Indeed Allah will admit those who have faith and do righteous deeds into gardens with streams running in them) It was said that the style was changed in this [verse], and the admission was attributed to Allah emphatically, in order to magnify the status of the believers. يُحَلَّوْنَ فِيها مِنْ أَساوِرَ ** (They will be adorned therein with bracelets) (أَساوِرَ) is the plural of أسورة, and أسورة is the plural of سوار. مِنْ ذَهَبٍ وَلُؤْلُؤاً ** (of gold and pearl) And it was read with nasb (النصب) and by omitting the first hamzah (الهمزة الأولى). وَلِباسُهُمْ فِيها حَرِيرٌ (and their dress therein will be silk.)

(٢٤) وَهُدُوا إِلَى الطَّيِّبِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ (24) And they are guided to good speech,

Al-Qummi said: It is التّوحيد and الإخلاص. وَهُدُوا إِلى صِراطِ الْحَمِيدِ ** He said: It is to الولاية.

In al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): By Allah, it is this matter (الامر) that you are upon.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: That refers to Hamzah, Ja'far, 'Ubaydah, Salman, Abu Dharr, al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad, and 'Ammar, and they were guided to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: There is no one to Whom praise (الحمد) is more beloved than Allah, the Mighty and Majestic is His mention.

Al-Qummi, narrated from Abu Basir, from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He said: I said to Him, "May I be made your ransom, make me yearn (for Paradise)." So He said: "O Abu Muhammad, indeed, among the least of the pleasures of Paradise is that its fragrance can be perceived from a distance of a thousand years of the world's distance. And indeed, the lowest in rank among the people of Paradise, if the two weighty ones (الثقلان), the jinn and mankind, were to alight in his abode, it would suffice them with food and drink, and nothing of what He possesses would be diminished. And indeed, the one with the easiest rank among the people of Paradise is he who enters Paradise and three gardens are raised for him. When he enters the lowest of them, he sees therein wives, servants, rivers, and fruits, as Allah wills, filling his eyes with delight and his heart with joy. When he thanks Allah and praises Him, it is said to him: 'Raise your head to the second garden, for therein is what is not in the first.' So he says: 'O Lord, grant me this.' So Allah, the Almighty, says: 'Perhaps if I grant it to you, you will ask Me for something else.' He says: 'Lord, this is it, this is it!' When he enters it, he thanks Allah alone." He said: "Then it is said: 'Open a door for him to Paradise,' and it is said to him: 'Raise your head.' Then a door of eternity (الخلد) is opened for him, and he sees multiples of what was in the previous ones. So, upon the multiplication of his joys, he says: 'Lord, to You belongs the praise (الحمد) that cannot be enumerated, since You have favored me with the Gardens and saved me from the Fires (النّيران).'" Abu Basir said: "So I wept and said to Him: 'May I be made your ransom, increase me (in knowledge).'" He said: "O Abu Muhammad, indeed in Paradise there is a river, and on its banks are maidens (جوار) growing. When the believer passes by a maiden who pleases him, he plucks her, and Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, causes another to grow in her place." I said: "May I be made your ransom, increase me." He said: "O Abu Muhammad, the believer will be married to eight hundred virgins (عذراء) and four thousand previously married women (ثيّب), and two wives from the wide-eyed houris (الحور العين)." I said: "May I be made your ransom, eight hundred virgins?" He said: "Yes, he does not lie with any of them except that he finds her thus (a virgin)." I said: "May I be made your ransom, from what were the wide-eyed houris (الحور العين) created?" He said: "From the luminous soil (تربة) of Paradise. He sees the marrow of her shanks from behind seventy garments. Her liver is his mirror, and his liver is her mirror." I said: "May I be made your ransom, do they have speech that they speak in Paradise?" He said: "Yes, speech that no creature has heard sweeter than it." I said: "What is it?" He said: "They say in soft voices: 'We are the eternal ones, so we shall not die. We are the blissful ones, so we shall not suffer hardship. We are the residents, so we shall not depart. We are the pleased ones, so we shall not be displeased. Blessed is he who was created for us, and blessed is he for whom we were created. And we are those such that if the braid (قَرْنَ) of one of us were hung in the atmosphere of the sky, it would eclipse the light of the eyes.'"

(25) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَيَصُدُّونَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ وَالْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ الَّذِي جَعَلْناهُ لِلنَّاسِ سَواءً الْعاكِفُ فِيهِ وَالْبادِ (25) Indeed, those who disbelieve and bar [people] from the way of Allah and the Sacred Mosque which We have appointed for all people, the resident and the visitor being equal therein.

(الْمُقِيمُ) (the resident) and (الطَّارِي) (the visitor). The predicate (خَبَر) of Inna (إِنَّ) is omitted because the end of the Ayah indicates it, meaning: they will be tormented (مُعَذَّبُونَ). And (سَوَاءً) was read with nasb (accusative case).

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Quraysh when they barred the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم from Mecca. Regarding His saying: سَواءً الْعاكِفُ فِيهِ وَالْبادِ ** (the resident and the visitor being equal therein), he said: The people of Mecca and those who come from other lands are equal therein; they are not prevented from lodging and entering the Sanctuary (الْحَرَم).

And in Nahj al-Balaghah, in a letter He wrote to Qutham ibn al-Abbas, who was His governor over Mecca: "And command the people of Mecca not to take rent from any resident, for Allah, the Glorified, says: سَواءً الْعاكِفُ فِيهِ وَالْبادِ ** (the resident and the visitor being equal therein). And the al-ʿĀkif (الْعَاكِفُ) is the one residing therein, and the al-Bād (الْبَادِ) is the one who performs Hajj to it from outside its people."

And in Qurb al-Isnad, narrated from him عليه السلام: He disliked renting out the houses of Mecca and recited this Ayah.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Indeed, Mu'awiyah was the first person who hung two door-leaves (مِصْرَاعَيْن) on his door in Mecca, thereby preventing the pilgrims (حَاجّ) of the House of Allah from what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, stated: سَواءً الْعاكِفُ فِيهِ وَالْبادِ ** (the resident and the visitor being equal therein). And when people arrived in Mecca, the visitor (الْبَادِي) would lodge with the resident (الْحَاضِر) until he completed his Hajj. And Mu'awiyah was the owner of the chain (السِّلْسِلَة) about which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: فِي سِلْسِلَةٍ ذَرْعُها سَبْعُونَ ذِراعاً (in a chain whose length is seventy cubits) [Q. 69:32]. And he was the Pharaoh of this Ummah. And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from him عليه السلام: The houses of Mecca did not have doors on any of them, and the first person who hung two door-leaves (الْمِصْرَاعَيْن) on his door was Mu'awiyah ibn Abi Sufyan. It is not proper for anyone to prevent the pilgrims from using any of the houses or their lodgings.

And in al-Ilal, narrated from him عليه السلام regarding this Ayah, he said: It was not proper for doors to be placed on the houses of Mecca, because the pilgrims have the right to lodge with them in their houses, in the courtyard of the house, until they complete their rituals (مَنَاسِكَهُم). And indeed, the first person who made doors for the houses of Mecca was Mu'awiyah. وَمَنْ يُرِدْ فِيهِ بِإِلْحادٍ ** (And whoever seeks deviation therein) — deviation (عُدُول) from the right path (الْقَصْد) — بِظُلْمٍ ** (with wrongdoing) — unjustly (بِغَيْرِ حَقّ). This is one of the instances where the object (مَفْعُول) is omitted so that it encompasses every possible instance (كُلّ مُتَنَاوِل). نُذِقْهُ مِنْ عَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ (We shall make him taste a painful punishment).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام regarding this Ayah: Whoever worships therein other than Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, or takes as a guardian therein other than the guardians (أَوْلِيَاء) of Allah, he is a wrongdoer (مُلْحِدٌ بِظُلْمٍ), and it is incumbent upon Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, to make him taste a painful punishment.

And narrated from him عليه السلام concerning it: Every wrongdoing (ظُلْم) is deviation (إِلْحَاد), and striking a servant without a sin is part of that deviation (الْإِلْحَاد). He was asked about the lowest form of deviation (أَدْنَى الْإِلْحَاد), and he replied: Indeed, arrogance (الْكِبْر) is the lowest of it. And therein [is also narrated]:

And in al-Ilal, narrated from him عليه السلام, he said: Every wrongdoing (ظُلْم) by which a man wrongs himself in Mecca, whether it be theft, or wronging someone, or anything of wrongdoing, I consider it deviation (إِلْحَادًا). For this reason, it was forbidden to reside in the Sanctuary (الْحَرَم).

And in al-Ilal, narrated from him عليه السلام: It was said to him that a predatory bird (سَبْعًا مِنْ سِبَاعِ الطَّيْر) was upon the Ka'bah, and nothing from the doves of the Sanctuary (حَمَامِ الْحَرَم) passed by it except that it struck it. So he said: "Set a trap for it and kill it, for it has committed deviation (أَلْحَدَ) in the Sanctuary (الْحَرَم)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him عليه السلام regarding this Ayah, he said: It was revealed concerning them when they entered the Ka'bah and made a covenant and agreement upon their disbelief and their denial of what was revealed concerning the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام. Thus, they committed deviation (أَلْحَدُوا) in the House by wronging the Messenger and his guardian (وَلِيّه). So, away with the wrongdoing people! And Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning whoever commits deviation against the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام and wrongs him.

(٢٦) وَإِذْ بَوَّأْنا لِإِبْراهِيمَ مَكانَ الْبَيْتِ أَنْ لا تُشْرِكْ بِي شَيْئاً وَطَهِّرْ بَيْتِيَ لِلطَّائِفِينَ وَالْقائِمِينَ وَالرُّكَّعِ السُّجُودِ (26) And when We assigned to Abraham the site of the House, [saying]: 'Do not associate anything with Me, and purify My House for those who circle around it, and those who stand [in prayer], and those who bow and prostrate.'

In al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Verily, Allah the Almighty says: وَطَهِّرْ بَيْتِيَ لِلطَّائِفِينَ وَالْقائِمِينَ وَالرُّكَّعِ السُّجُودِ (and purify My House for those who circle around it, and those who stand [in prayer], and those who bow and prostrate). Thus, it is incumbent upon the servant (العبد) not to enter Mecca (مكّة) except while he is pure (طاهر), having washed his sweat (عرقه) and filth (الأذى), and having purified himself (تطهّر)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "Verily, Allah the Almighty has one hundred and twenty mercies (رحمة) around the Ka'bah (الكعبة): sixty of them are for those who perform the circumambulation (للطّائفين), forty are for those who pray (للمصلّين), and twenty are for those who look (للنّاظرين)." And other narrations (اخبار اخر) related to this verse have already passed in Surah al-Baqarah (سورة البقرة).

(27) وَأَذِّنْ فِي النَّاسِ And proclaim among them بِالْحَجِ with the Hajj, by inviting them to it. يَأْتُوكَ رِجالاً They will come to you on foot, the plural of راجل (pedestrian). (27) (And proclaim the Hajj among the people) (They will come to you on foot)

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He recited رجّالاً with the doubling (التشديد) and the ḍammah (الضم).

وَعَلى كُلِّ ضامِرٍ and on every lean camel, meaning, and riders upon every lean camel, which the distance of the journey has exhausted and made lean. يَأْتِينَ coming, is an attribute (صفة) for ضامر (lean camel), carried according to its meaning. And the reading يأتون (they come) is an attribute for the men (الرّجال) and the riders (الرّكبان), or it is a new sentence (استيناف). And he attributed it in al-Majma' to al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). مِنْ كُلِّ فَجٍ from every deep ravine, path (طريق). عَمِيقٍ deep, distant in its extremities. (and on every lean camel, coming from every deep ravine)

In al-Kafi and al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: When Ibrahim and Isma'il (peace be upon them) were commanded to build the House (البَيت), and its construction was completed, Ibrahim (peace be upon him) sat upon a corner (ركن) and then called out: "Come to the Hajj!" Had He called out: "Come, all of you, to the Hajj!" (هلمّوا الى الحجّ), only those who were created as human beings (انسيّا مخلوقاً) on that day would have performed Hajj. But He called out: "Come! Come! The Hajj! The Hajj!" So the people responded with the talbiyah (لبيّك داعي الله لبيّك داعي الله) while they were in the loins of men. Thus, whoever responded with the talbiyah ten times performed Hajj ten times, and whoever responded five times performed Hajj five times, and whoever responded more, it was according to that number, and whoever responded once performed Hajj once, and whoever did not respond with the talbiyah did not perform Hajj.

And in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: Indeed, when Allah, majestic is His glory (جلّ جلاله), commanded Ibrahim (peace be upon him) to proclaim the Hajj among the people, He stood upon the Station (المقام), and it was raised with Him until He was opposite Mount Abu Qubays (أبي قبيس). Then He proclaimed the Hajj among the people, and He made those in the loins of men and the wombs of women hear it until the Hour is established.

And al-Qummi said: When Ibrahim (peace be upon him) finished building the House (البيت), Allah commanded Him to proclaim the Hajj among the people. So He said: "O Lord, my voice will not reach." Allah said: "The proclamation (الأذان) is upon you, and the conveyance (البلاغ) is upon Me." He ascended the Station (المقام), which at that time was attached to the House (البيت). The Station was raised with Him until it was taller than the mountains. He called out, placing His finger in His ear, and turned His face East and West, saying: "O people! The Hajj to the Ancient House (البيت العتيق) has been prescribed for you, so respond to your Lord!" So they responded to Him from beneath the seven seas (البحور السّبع), and from between the East and the West, to the end of the earth (منقطع التراب) from all the corners of the earth, and from the loins of men and the wombs of women, with the talbiyah: لبّيك اللهم لبّيك (Here I am, O Allah, here I am). Do you not see them coming, responding with the talbiyah? Thus, whoever performs Hajj from that day until the Day of Resurrection is among those who responded to Allah. And His saying: فِيهِ آياتٌ بَيِّناتٌ مَقامُ إِبْراهِيمَ ** (In it are manifest signs: Abraham’s Station) means the call (نداء) of Ibrahim (peace be upon him) upon the Station (المقام).

In al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Indeed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم resided in Medina for ten years and did not perform Hajj. Then Allah the Almighty revealed: وَأَذِّنْ فِي النَّاسِ بِالْحَجِ (And proclaim the Hajj among the people) the Ayah. So He commanded the callers (المؤذّنين) to proclaim with their loudest voices that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would perform Hajj in that year of His. Thus, those present in Medina, the people of al-Awali (أهل العوالي), and the Bedouins (الاعراب) learned of it, and they gathered for the Hajj of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. They were merely followers, observing what He was commanded to do so they might follow it, or if He did something, they would do it. (The rest of) the narrative (الحديث).

(28) لِيَشْهَدُوا ** That they may attend/be present مَنافِعَ لَهُمْ ** religious and worldly benefits for them.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was said to him, "If only you would rest your body from the litter (المحمل)." He (peace be upon him) said: "I love to witness the benefits which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, mentioned: That they may witness benefits for them. Indeed, no one witnesses them except that Allah benefits him. As for you (pilgrims), you return forgiven, and as for others (those who did not attend), they are protected regarding their families and their wealth."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was being circumambulated around the Ka'bah in a litter (محمل) while he was severely ill. And whenever he reached the Yemeni Corner (الرّكن اليماني), he commanded them to place him on the ground. Then he would take his hand out of the opening (كوّة) of the litter until he dragged it on the ground, then he would say, "Lift me up." When he did that repeatedly in every circuit (شوط), it was said to him, "O son of the Messenger of Allah, this is difficult for you." He said: "I heard Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, saying: لِيَشْهَدُوا مَنافِعَ لَهُمْ ." It was asked, "Benefits of the world or benefits of the Hereafter?" He replied, "All of them (الكلّ)."

And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): The benefits of the Hereafter are pardon (العفو) and forgiveness (المغفرة).

And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): And the reason (علّة) for Hajj is the delegation (الوفادة) to Allah, the Exalted, and the seeking of increase (الزّيادة), and the departure from everything one has committed, so that one may be repentant (تائباً) for what has passed, and commencing anew (مستأنفاً) for what is to come. And what it contains of the expenditure of wealth (استخراج الأموال), the fatigue of bodies (تعب الأبدان), and the restraining of them from desires (الشهوات) and pleasures (اللّذات), and drawing near to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, through worship (العبادة), and submission (الخضوع), humility (الاستكانة), and lowliness (الذلّ), being exposed (شاخصاً) to heat and cold, security and fear, constantly (دائباً) and perpetually (دائم) engaged in that. And what is in that for all creation of benefits (المنافع), and desire (الرّغبة) and awe (الرهبة) directed towards Allah, the Exalted. And from it is the abandonment of hardness of heart (قساوة القلب), the audacity of souls (جسارة الأنفس), the forgetting of remembrance (نسيان الذّكر), and the cutting off of hope (الرجاء) and expectation (الأمل), and the renewal of rights (تجديد الحقوق) and the protection (خطر) of souls from corruption (الفساد). And the benefit of those in the East of the earth and its West, and those on land and sea, whether they perform Hajj or not—for the merchant (تاجر), the importer (جالب), the seller (بايع), the buyer (مشتر), the earner (كاسب), and the poor (مسكين)—and the fulfillment of the needs of the people of the regions (الأطراف) and the places where it is possible for them to gather, likewise: لِيَشْهَدُوا مَنافِعَ لَهُمْ.

And he added in another narration: Along with what it contains of understanding the religion (التفقّه) and the transmission of the reports of the Imams (peace be upon them) to every region (صقع) and direction (ناحية), just as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: فَلَوْ لا نَفَرَ مِنْ كُلِّ فِرْقَةٍ مِنْهُمْ طائِفَةٌ لِيَتَفَقَّهُوا فِي الدِّينِ وَلِيُنْذِرُوا قَوْمَهُمْ إِذا رَجَعُوا إِلَيْهِمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَحْذَرُونَ (122) (Why does not a group from every division of them mobilize, to become learned in the religion, and to warn their people when they return to them, so that they may beware?)

وَيَذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللهِ فِي أَيَّامٍ مَعْلُوماتٍ عَلى ما رَزَقَهُمْ مِنْ بَهِيمَةِ الْأَنْعامِ ** (and mention the Name of Allah on designated days over the cattle He has provided them.) It was said that this means at the time of slaughtering them. And it was said that the mention (الذكر) is a metonymy (كنّى) for the slaughter (الذّبح) because the former is inseparable from the latter.

In al-Awali, narrated from the two of them (peace be upon them): It is the Takbir (saying Allahu Akbar) following fifteen prayers, the first of which is the Dhuhr (noon) prayer of the Eid.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The like of it.

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: 'Ali (peace be upon him) said regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: and mention the Name of Allah on designated days, He (Ali) said: "The days of the Ten (أيّام العشر)." Narrated from him (al-Sadiq, peace be upon him), he said: "They are the Days of al-Tashriq (أيّام التشريق)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The designated (المعلومات) and the numbered (المعدودات) are one, and they are the Days of al-Tashriq (أيّام التشريق).

And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from him, from his father, and in a narration from 'Ali (peace be upon him): Indeed, the designated days (الأيّام المعلومات) are the days of the Ten (أيّام العشر), and the numbered days (المعدودات) are the Days of al-Tashriq (أيّام التّشريق).

And in al-Jawami', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, the designated days (الأيّام المعلومات) are the Day of Sacrifice (يوم النّحر) and the three days following it (which are) the Days of al-Tashriq (أيّام التشريق), and the numbered days (الأيّام المعدودات) are the Ten of Dhul-Hijjah (عشر ذي الحجة).

فَكُلُوا مِنْها وَأَطْعِمُوا الْبائِسَ الْفَقِيرَ ** (So eat from them and feed the distressed poor.) He whom distress (بؤس) and hardship (شدّة) have afflicted.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He is the chronically ill (الزّمن) who is unable to go out due to his chronic illness (زمانته).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The distressed (البائس) is the poor (الفقير).

(٢٩) ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ ** (Then let them remove their untidiness) Then let them remove their filth (وسخهم) by clipping the nails, trimming the mustache, shaving the head, and similar actions. وَلْيُوفُوا نُذُورَهُمْ ** (and fulfill their vows) (Meaning) the rituals (مناسك) of their Hajj. And it was recited (وقرء) with a kasrah on the lām in both (verbs) and with a tashdīd on the fāʾ.

In al-Kāfī and al-Faqīh, narrated from al-Sādiq (peace be upon him): Al-Tafath (التفث) is shaving (الحلق) and what is on the skin of the human being.

And narrated from al-Ridā (peace be upon him): Al-Tafath (التفث) is clipping the nails, removing the filth (الوسخ), and casting off the Ihrām from oneself.

And in al-Faqīh, narrated from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him): Al-Tafath (التفث) is the man's removal of perfume (الطيب). When he completes his ritual (نسكه), perfume becomes lawful for him.

And in al-Kāfī, narrated from al-Sādiq (peace be upon him): Part of al-Tafath (التفث) is that you speak ugly words (كلام قبيح) during your Ihrām. When you enter Mecca and circumambulate the House (البيت), you speak good words (كلام طيّب), and that serves as an expiation (كفّارة).

And narrated from ‘Abd Allāh ibn Sinān, from Dhurayh al-Muhāribī, who said: I said to Abū ‘Abd Allāh (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah has commanded me in His Book with a command, and I love to know it." He said: "And what is that?" I said: "The Almighty's saying: **ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ وَلْيُوفُوا نُذُورَهُمْ ** (Then let them remove their untidiness and fulfill their vows)."

He (peace be upon him) said: " لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ (Let them remove their untidiness) means meeting the Imām (لقاء الإِمام). وَلْيُوفُوا نُذُورَهُمْ ** (And fulfill their vows) means those rituals (المَناسِك)."

‘Abd Allāh ibn Sinān said: Then I came to Abū ‘Abd Allāh (peace be upon him) and said: "May I be made your ransom (جُعلت فداك), the Almighty's saying: ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ وَلْيُوفُوا نُذُورَهُمْ ?" He said: "Trimming the mustache, clipping the nails, and what resembles that."

I said: "May I be made your ransom, Dhurayh al-Muhāribī narrated to me from you that you said to him: لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ (Let them remove their untidiness) means meeting the Imām (لقاء الإِمام), and وَلْيُوفُوا نُذُورَهُمْ (and fulfill their vows) means those rituals (المناسك)." He said: "He spoke the truth, and you spoke the truth. Indeed, the Quran has an outward (ظاهراً) and an inward (باطناً), and who can bear what Dhurayh bears?"

I say: The point of commonality between the exegesis (التفسير) and the esoteric interpretation (التأويل) is purification (التطهير), for one of them is purification from apparent filth (الأوساخ الظاهرة) and the other is purification from ignorance (الجهل) and blindness (العمى). He said in al-Faqīh: The meaning of al-Tafath (التّفث) is everything that the narrations have mentioned.

And in al-Kāfī, narrated from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him): He says, and He sees the people in Mecca and what they do, actions like the actions of the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية). "By Allah, they were not commanded with this, and they were only commanded that لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ وَلْيُوفُوا (they remove their untidiness and fulfill) نُذُورَهُمْ ** (their vows) so that they pass by us, inform us of their allegiance (ولايتهم), and offer us their support (نصرتهم)."

وَلْيَطَّوَّفُوا بِالْبَيْتِ الْعَتِيقِ ** (and circumambulate the Ancient House) And it was recited (وقرء) with a kasrah on the lām.

And in al-Kāfī, narrated from al-Sādiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and he said: "It is the circumambulation of the women (طواف النّساء)."

And narrated from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him): He was asked why Allah named the House al-ʿAtīq (العتيق - the Ancient/Free). He said: "It is a free House (بيت حرّ), ancient/free from people; no one has ever owned it."

And in al-Mahāsin, al-ʿIlal, and al-Qummī, narrated from al-Sādiq (peace be upon him): The House was named al-Bayt al-ʿAtīq (البيت العتيق) because it was freed (أعتق) from the Deluge (الغرق).

(٣٠) ذلِكَ ** That refers to the matter (الأَمر) mentioned. And this (ذلِكَ) and its likes are used to separate between two discourses. وَمَنْ يُعَظِّمْ حُرُماتِ اللهِ ** (and whoever venerates the sanctities of Allah) refers to His rulings (أحكامه) and that which is unlawful to violate (هتكه). فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَهُ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِ ** (it is better for him with his Lord) meaning in reward (ثوابا). وَأُحِلَّتْ لَكُمُ الْأَنْعامُ إِلَّا ما يُتْلى عَلَيْكُمْ ** (The cattle are lawful for you, except what is recited to you) such as the dead animal (الميتة) and that over which the name of other than Allah has been invoked (وما اهلّ به لغير الله). So do not forbid from them anything other than what Allah has forbidden, such as the Bahirah (البحيرة) and the Sa'ibah (السّائبة). فَاجْتَنِبُوا الرِّجْسَ مِنَ الْأَوْثانِ وَاجْتَنِبُوا قَوْلَ الزُّورِ ** (So shun the filth of idols, and shun false speech,) So shun the filth (الرّجس) which is the idols (الأوثان), just as impurities (الأنجاس) are shunned, and [shun] every fabrication (افتراء).

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: الرِّجْسَ مِنَ الْأَوْثانِ ** (the filth of idols) is chess (الشطرنج), and قَوْلَ الزُّورِ ** (false speech) is singing (الغناء).

And it is added in al-Majma': and other types of gambling (القمار) and other distracting speeches (الأقوال الملهية). And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: False testimony (شهادة الزّور) was equated with associating partners with Allah (الشرك بالله). Then He recited this Ayah.

(31) حُنَفاءَ لِلَّهِ (Being upright for Allah) Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Meaning, pure ones (طاهرين). غَيْرَ مُشْرِكِينَ بِهِ (not associating anything with Him) In al-Tawhid, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about it and about al-Ḥanīfiyyah (الحنيفة). He said: "It is the innate disposition (الفطرة) upon which Allah created mankind. 'There is no altering Allah’s creation' (لا تبديل لخلق الله)." He said: "Allah created them upon the knowledge (المعرفة)." وَمَنْ يُشْرِكْ بِاللهِ فَكَأَنَّما خَرَّ مِنَ السَّماءِ ** (and whoever associates partners with Allah is as though he has fallen from the sky) Because he has fallen from the peak (أوج) of faith (الإيمان) to the lowest point (حضيض) of disbelief (الكفر). فَتَخْطَفُهُ الطَّيْرُ ** (and the birds snatch him away) For the destructive whims (الأهواء المردية) scatter his thoughts (أفكاره). And it was recited with the doubling (تشديد) of the tā’ (التّاء). أَوْ تَهْوِي بِهِ الرِّيحُ فِي مَكَانٍ (or the wind carries him away to a place) سَحِيقٍ ** (far away) Distant (بعيد). For Satan has cast him into error (الضّلالة).

(32) ذلِكَ ** That is the matter (الامر). وَمَنْ يُعَظِّمْ شَعائِرَ اللهِ ** (And whoever venerates the sacraments of Allah)—meaning, the signs (اعلام) of His religion—فَإِنَّها مِنْ تَقْوَى الْقُلُوبِ ** (that is indeed the piety of the hearts.) Al-Qummi said: [It means] the veneration (تعظيم) and quality (جودتها) of the sacrificial animal (البدن).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The reward (الجزاء) is only multiplied (مضاعفاً) for what is less than the large sacrificial animal (البدنة). But when it reaches the large sacrificial animal (البدنة), it is not multiplied, because it is the greatest thing that can be [offered]. Allah the Almighty said: وَمَنْ يُعَظِّمْ شَعائِرَ اللهِ فَإِنَّها مِنْ تَقْوَى الْقُلُوبِ. (And whoever venerates the sacraments of Allah—that is indeed the piety of the hearts.)

And narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding the story of the Farewell Pilgrimage (حجّة الوداع): The sacrificial animals (الهدى) that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم brought were sixty-four or sixty-six, and ‘Ali (peace be upon him) brought thirty-four or thirty-six.

(33) لَكُمْ فِيها مَنافِعُ إِلى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى (33) You have in them benefits until a specified time.

In al-Kafi and al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: "If he needs its back, he may ride it without treating it harshly (يُعَنِّف), and if it has milk, he may milk it with a milking that does not exhaust it (يُنْهِكُهَا)."

ثُمَّ مَحِلُّها إِلَى الْبَيْتِ الْعَتِيقِ (then their place of sacrifice is toward the Ancient House.)

Al-Qummi said: The budn (sacrificial animals) are ridden by the muhrim from the place where he enters the state of ihram, without harming them or treating them harshly (مُعَنِّف). And if they have milk, he may drink from their milk until the Day of Sacrifice (يوم النّحر).

(٣٤) وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ اهل دين جَعَلْنا مَنْسَكاً ** (And for every nation We have appointed a ritual) meaning, people of a religion (أهل دين). جَعَلْنا مَنْسَكاً ** (We have appointed a ritual) — meaning, an act of devotion (متعبّداً) and a sacrifice (قرباناً) by which they draw near to Allah. And it was read with a kasrah (مَنسِكًا), meaning a place of ritual (موضع نسك). لِيَذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللهِ ** (so that they may mention Allah’s Name) — to the exclusion of others, and that they make their ritual (نسكهم) for His Face/sake. He made the appointment (الجعل) conditional upon this, building it upon the premise that the purpose of the rituals (المناسك) is the remembrance of the Worshipped One (المعبود). عَلى ما رَزَقَهُمْ مِنْ بَهِيمَةِ الْأَنْعامِ ** (over what He has provided them of the cattle) — when slaughtering them. فَإِلهُكُمْ إِلهٌ واحِدٌ فَلَهُ أَسْلِمُوا (So your God is the One God; therefore submit to Him) — make the drawing near (التقرب) and the remembrance (الذكر) sincere, and do not mix it with association (بالاشراك). وَبَشِّرِ الْمُخْبِتِينَ. (And give good news to the humble) Al-Qummi said: the worshippers (العابدين).

(٣٥) الَّذِينَ إِذا ذُكِرَ اللهُ وَجِلَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ ** (35) those whose hearts tremble when Allah is mentioned out of awe (هيبة) of Him, due to the shining (لأشراق) of the rays (اشعّة) of His Majesty (جلاله) upon them. وَالصَّابِرِينَ عَلى ما أَصابَهُمْ ** and who are patient with whatever afflicts them of calamities (المصائب). وَالْمُقِيمِي الصَّلاةِ ** and maintain the prayer in establishing (إفادتها) it. وَمِمَّا رَزَقْناهُمْ يُنْفِقُونَ ** and spend out of what We have provided them. in ways (وجوه) of goodness (الخير).

(٣٦) وَالْبُدْنَ جَعَلْناها لَكُمْ مِنْ شَعائِرِ اللهِ لَكُمْ فِيها خَيْرٌ ** (36) And the camels We have appointed them for you as part of Allah’s sacraments. There is good for you in them. (They are) the plural of بدنة (sacrificial animal). (The good is) religious and worldly benefits. فَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللهِ عَلَيْها صَوافَ ** So mention Allah’s Name over them, lined up. (Meaning) standing, having lined up their forelegs and hind legs. Al-Qummi said: It is slaughtered while standing. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That is when they are lined up for slaughter; their forelegs are tied between the hoof (الخفّ) and the knee (الرّكبة). And He (Imam al-Sadiq) read (صوافن) with the nūn (ن) and attributed it (the reading). In al-Majma', attributed to Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): And it (صوافن) is derived from the ṣafn (صفن) of the horse when it stands on three (legs) and on the tip of the hoof (سنبك) of the fourth, because the sacrificial animal (البدنة) has one of its forelegs tied, so it stands on three (legs). فَإِذا وَجَبَتْ جُنُوبُها. When their flanks have fallen. In al-Kafi and al-Ma'ani, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: When it falls upon the ground. فَكُلُوا مِنْها وَأَطْعِمُوا الْقانِعَ وَالْمُعْتَرَّ Then eat from them, and feed the contented and the supplicant. He said: The القانع (contented) is he who is satisfied with what you have given him, and is neither displeased nor frowns (يَكْلَحُ) nor twists his cheek (شِدْقَهُ) in anger. And the الْمُعْتَرَّ (supplicant) is the one who passes by you so that you may feed him. In al-Ma'ani, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): Feed your family one-third, feed the القانع (contented) one-third, and feed the المسكين (needy) one-third. It was asked: Is the المسكين (needy) the beggar (السائل)? He said: Yes. And the القانع (contented) is satisfied with what you send to him, be it a piece (البضعة) or more. And the المعترّ (supplicant) approaches you but does not ask you. In al-Majma', narrated from Them (peace be upon them): It is proper that one feeds (his family) one-third of it, gives the القانع (contented) and the المعترّ (supplicant) one-third of it, and gifts the remaining third to his friends. كَذلِكَ سَخَّرْناها لَكُمْ ** Thus have We subjected them to you, despite their size and strength, so that you take them submissively, tie them up, restrain them while their legs are lined up, and then stab them in their throats (لبّاتها). لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ ** So that you may give thanks. For Our favors upon you, through drawing near (to Us) and sincerity.

(٣٧) لَنْ يَنالَ اللهَ لُحُومُها وَلا دِماؤُها وَلَكِنْ يَنالُهُ التَّقْوى مِنْكُمْ (Neither their flesh nor their blood reaches Allah, but your piety reaches Him.) لَنْ يَنالَ اللهَ (It will not reach Allah) means: It will not attain His pleasure, nor will it fall into the place of acceptance from Him. لُحُومُها (their flesh) refers to that which is given in charity. وَلا دِماؤُها (nor their blood) refers to that which is spilled by slaughtering (بالنّحر), merely because they are flesh and blood. وَلكِنْ يَنالُهُ التَّقْوى مِنْكُمْ (but your piety reaches Him) means: But what reaches Him is the piety of your hearts that accompanies it, which calls you to the command of Allah, to His glorification, to drawing near to Him, and to sincerity (الإِخلاص) towards Him.

In al-Jawami', it is narrated: In the Age of Ignorance (الجاهليّة), when they slaughtered, they used to smear the House (البيت) with blood. When the Muslims performed the Hajj, they intended to do the same, so this [verse] was revealed.

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked what the reason (علّة) for the sacrifice (الأضحية) was. He said: "Indeed, it is forgiven for its owner at the first drop of its blood that falls to the earth, and so that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, may know who fears Him unseen (بالغيب)." Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: لَنْ يَنالَ اللهَ لُحُومُها ** (Neither their flesh nor their blood reaches Allah) — the Ayah. Then He (peace be upon him) said: "Look how Allah accepted the offering (قربان) of Habil and rejected the offering (قربان) of Qabil."

كَذلِكَ سَخَّرَها لَكُمْ لِتُكَبِّرُوا اللهَ عَلَى مَا هَدَاكُمْ وَبَشِّرِ الْمُحْسِنِينَ (Thus has He subjected them to you, so that you may magnify Allah for guiding you. And give good news to the virtuous.) كَذلِكَ سَخَّرَها لَكُمْ (Thus has He subjected them to you) He repeated this [phrase] as a reminder of the favor and as a reason for what follows it: لِتُكَبِّرُوا اللهَ (so that you may magnify Allah) so that you may recognize His greatness through His power over that which none other than Him has power, and thus declare His Oneness (فتوحّدوه) through majesty (بالكبرياء).

And al-Qummi said: The Takbir (التكبير) during the Days of Tashriq (أيّام التشريق) in the prayers at Mina is after fifteen prayers, and in the major cities (الأمصار) it is after ten prayers. عَلى ما هَداكُمْ (for guiding you) meaning: He guided you to the way of subjecting them and the manner of drawing near [to Him] through them. وَبَشِّرِ الْمُحْسِنِينَ (And give good news to the virtuous) meaning: those who are sincere (المخلصون) in what they perform and what they avoid.

(38) إِنَّ اللهَ يُدافِعُ عَنِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (Indeed Allah defends those who have faith.) the malice (غائلة) of the polytheists. And it was recited (وقرء) as يَدْفَعُ (He repels). إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ خَوَّانٍ ** (Indeed Allah does not like any treacherous ingrate.) treacherous (خَوَّانٍ) in the trust (امانة) of Allah, ungrateful (كَفُورٍ) for His favor, like one who seeks nearness to idols with his sacrifice (بذبيحته).

(39) أُذِنَ ** (Permission is granted) Permission (رخّص) was granted. And it was recited (وقرء) with the opening (بفتح) of the hamzah (الهمزة) [i.e., أَذِنَ], meaning Allah [granted permission]. لِلَّذِينَ يُقاتَلُونَ ** (to those who are being fought) [meaning those who fight] the polytheists, i.e., in fighting (في القتال). [The object] was omitted because the context indicates it. And it was recited (وقرء) with the opening (بفتح) of the tā’ (التّاء) [i.e., يُقَاتِلُونَ], meaning: to those whom the polytheists fight. **بِأَنَّهُمْ ظُلِمُوا ** (because they have been wronged.) due to the fact that they were wronged.

In al-Majma’, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was not commanded to fight, nor was permission granted to Him for it, until Gabriel (peace be upon him) descended with this Ayah and girded Him with a sword. And therein [it is narrated]: The polytheists used to harm the Muslims. A person who was wounded (مشجوج) or beaten (مضروب) would constantly come to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and complain about that to Him. He would say to them: "Be patient, for I have not been commanded to fight." Until He emigrated (هاجر), then Allah sent down this Ayah upon Him in Medina, and it is the first Ayah revealed concerning fighting (القتال).

And al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Ali, Ja’far, and Hamzah, and then it became general (جرت).

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The general populace (العامّة) say that it was revealed concerning the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم when Quraysh expelled Him from Mecca. However, it is [truly] the al-Qā’im (القائم) when He emerges seeking the blood of al-Husayn (peace be upon them both), and He will say: "We are the guardians of the blood (أولياء الدّم) and the seekers of vengeance (طلّاب التّرة)." وَإِنَّ اللهَ عَلى نَصْرِهِمْ لَقَدِيرٌ ** (and Allah is indeed able to help them.) This is a promise to them of victory (بالنّصر), just as He promised to repel the harm (أذى) of the disbelievers from them.

(٤٠) الَّذِينَ أُخْرِجُوا مِنْ دِيارِهِمْ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ إِلَّا أَنْ يَقُولُوا رَبُّنَا اللهُ ** (40) those who were expelled from their homes unjustly, merely because they said, 'Our Lord is Allah.' Meaning that they were not expelled except because of their saying: رَبُّنَا اللهُ.** (Our Lord is Allah.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and Ali عليه السلام, and Hamzah, and Ja'far, and it applies to al-Husayn عليه السلام.

And al-Qummi (narrated): Al-Husayn عليه السلام said it when Yazid sought him to carry him to Sham (الشام), so he fled to Kufa (الكوفة) and was killed at al-Taff (الطّف).

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning the Emigrants (المهاجرين) and applies to the Family of Muhammad عليهم السلام **الَّذِينَ أُخْرِجُوا مِنْ دِيارِهِمْ ** (those who were expelled from their homes) and were intimidated.

And in al-Manaqib, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "We are those concerning whom it was revealed."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in the tradition of al-Zubayri: "That is for a people for whom it is not lawful except for them, and none undertakes that except one who is from them." Then He mentioned the conditions in detail in a tradition recorded in the book of al-Jihad (الجهاد). Whoever desires it should seek it therein.

وَلَوْ لا دَفْعُ اللهِ النَّاسَ بَعْضَهُمْ بِبَعْضٍ ** (40) Were it not that Allah checks the people, some by means of others, by empowering the believers over the disbelievers. And it was read (دفاع) [instead of دفع].

لَهُدِّمَتْ ** (40) surely monasteries And it was read with takhfif (التّخفيف) [as لَهُدِمَتْ], meaning they would be ruined due to the domination of the polytheists over the followers of the religions.

صَوامِعُ ** (40) and churches, The monasteries (صوامع) of the monks (الرهبانية).

وَبِيَعٌ ** (40) and synagogues, And the churches (بيع) of the Christians (النّصارى).

وَصَلَواتٌ ** (40) and mosques, And the synagogues (كنائس) of the Jews (اليهود). It was said they were named thus because prayer (تصلّي) is performed therein. And it was said that its origin is thulutha (ثلوثا) with the tri-dotted tha (الثّاء المثلّثة) in Hebrew, meaning the place of prayer (المصلّى), and it was then Arabized (عرّبت).

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He read صلوات with the dammah (ضمّ) on the sad (الصاد) and the lam (اللام).

وَمَساجِدُ ** (40) and mosques, And the mosques (مساجد) of the Muslims (المسلمين).

**يُذْكَرُ فِيهَا اسْمُ اللهِ كَثِيراً وَلَيَنْصُرَنَّ اللهُ مَنْ يَنْصُرُهُ إِنَّ اللهَ لَقَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ ** (40) wherein Allah's Name is often mentioned, would surely have been demolished. Allah will surely help him who helps Him. Indeed Allah is all-strong, all-mighty. Nothing can prevent Him.

(٤١) الَّذِينَ إِنْ مَكَّنَّاهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ أَقامُوا الصَّلاةَ وَآتَوُا الزَّكاةَ وَأَمَرُوا بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَنَهَوْا عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ وَلِلَّهِ عاقِبَةُ الْأُمُورِ (41) (They are) those who, if We establish them in the land, maintain the prayer and give the zakat, and enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong. And with Allah rests the outcome of all matters.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "This [verse] is for the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) until the end of the Ayah. And al-Mahdi (peace be upon him) and his companions—Allah will grant them dominion over the Easts of the earth and its Wests, and He will make the religion manifest. And Allah will cause innovations (البدَع) and falsehood (الباطل) to die through him and his companions, just as the wretched ones (الشقاة) caused the truth (الحقّ) to die, until injustice (الظّلم) is not seen anywhere. And they will enjoin what is right (بِالْمَعْرُوفِ) and forbid what is wrong (عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ)."

And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): "We are them."

And in al-Manaqib, narrated from al-Kazim and his grandfather, the Master of the Martyrs (peace be upon them both): "This [verse] is concerning us, the People of the House (اهل البيت)."

(٤٢) وَإِنْ يُكَذِّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ وَعادٌ وَثَمُودُ (42) If they deny you, [know that] before them the people of Noah, ‘Ād, and Thamūd denied [their apostles]. (٤٣) وَقَوْمُ إِبْراهِيمَ وَقَوْمُ لُوطٍ (43) and the people of Abraham, and the people of Lot, (٤٤) وَأَصْحابُ مَدْيَنَ ** (44) and the inhabitants of Midian. Consolation for the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. وَكُذِّبَ مُوسى ** and Moses was denied. It was said that the structure (النظم) was changed therein because his people did not deny him; rather, the Copts (القبط) denied him. And because his denial was more heinous, and His signs (آياته) were greater and more widespread. فَأْمَلَيْتُ لِلْكافِرِينَ (I gave the faithless a respite) So I granted them respite until their appointed terms (آجالهم) came to an end. ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُهُمْ فَكَيْفَ كانَ نَكِيرِ ** then I seized them. So how was My retribution? My disapproval (إنكاري) of them by changing the blessing into affliction (محنة), life into destruction (هلاكاً), and civilization into ruin (خراباً).

(٤٥) فَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ أَهْلَكْناها (How many a town We destroyed) by destroying its people. And it was read (وقرء): أهلكتها (I destroyed it). وَهِيَ ظالِمَةٌ ** (while it was doing wrong) meaning, its people. فَهِيَ خاوِيَةٌ عَلى عُرُوشِها ** (so it is fallen upon its roofs) meaning, its walls have fallen upon its ceilings. وَبِئْرٍ مُعَطَّلَةٍ ** (and [how many] a deserted well) from which water is not drawn due to the destruction of its people. وَقَصْرٍ مَشِيدٍ ** (and a lofty palace!) meaning, high, which We emptied of its inhabitants.

In al-Majma' and in Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), regarding His saying: **وَبِئْرٍ مُعَطَّلَةٍ ** (and a deserted well), meaning: And how many a scholar is there to whom people do not return and whose knowledge is not benefited from.

In al-Ikmal, al-Ma'ani, and al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): The deserted well (البئر المعطّلة) is the Silent Imam (الإمام الصّامت), and the lofty palace (القصر المشيد) is the Speaking Imam (الامام الناطق).

I say: The Silent Imam (الإِمام الصامت) was only alluded to by the well (البئر) because He is the source of knowledge, which is the cause of life for souls, despite His concealment except from those who approach Him, just as the well is the source of water, which is the cause of life for bodies, despite its concealment except from those who approach it. And His silence was alluded to by the desertion (التعطيل) due to the lack of benefit derived from His knowledge. And the Speaking Imam (الامام الناطق) was alluded to by the lofty palace (القصر المشيد) due to His manifestation, the elevation of His station, and the proclamation of His remembrance.

In al-Ma'ani, in a disconnected narration (مقطوعاً): The Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) is the lofty palace (القصر المشيد), and the deserted well (البئر المعطّلة) is Fatimah, and her children are deserted (معطّلين) from sovereignty (الملك).

And al-Qummi said: It is a parable for the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). وَبِئْرٍ مُعَطَّلَةٍ ** (and a deserted well) is that from which water is not drawn, and He is the Imam who has become absent, so knowledge is not acquired from Him until the time of His manifestation. And the lofty palace (القصر المشيد) is the elevated one, and it is a parable for the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) and the Imams (peace be upon them) and their virtues which are spread throughout the worlds and shine upon the world. And this is His saying: لِيُظْهِرَهُ عَلَى الدِّينِ كُلِّهِ* ** (that He may make it prevail over all religions). And the poet said:
