A deserted well and a lofty palace, A beautiful parable for the Family of Muhammad. The palace is their glory which cannot be surpassed, And the well is their knowledge which is never exhausted.

(٤٦) أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (46) Have they not travelled in the land It was said: This is an urging for them to travel so that they may see the downfall (مصارع) of those who were destroyed, and thus take heed. In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Its meaning is: Have they not looked into the Quran? فَتَكُونَ لَهُمْ قُلُوبٌ يَعْقِلُونَ بِها ** so that they may have hearts with which to exercise their reason [Hearts with which to exercise their reason] regarding what must be reasoned, أَوْ آذانٌ يَسْمَعُونَ بِها or ears with which to hear? [Or ears with which to hear] regarding what must be heard. فَإِنَّها لا تَعْمَى الْأَبْصارُ وَلكِنْ تَعْمَى الْقُلُوبُ الَّتِي فِي الصُّدُورِ Indeed, it is not the eyes that are blind, but blind are the hearts which are in the breasts. [Blind are the hearts which are in the breasts] from taking heed (عن الاعتبار). That is, the defect is not in their senses (مشاعرهم), but rather their intellects (عقولهم) have become corrupted (أنفت) due to following desires (باتّباع الهوى) and being engrossed in blind imitation (الانهماك في التقليد).

In al-Tawhid and al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): Indeed, the servant has four eyes: two eyes with which he sees the affairs of his religion (دينه) and his worldly life (دنياه), and two eyes with which he sees the affairs of his Hereafter (آخرته). So, if Allah wills good for a servant, He opens for him the two eyes that are in his heart, and with them he sees the Unseen (الغيب) and the affairs of his Hereafter. But if Allah wills other than that for him, He leaves the heart as it is (بما فيه).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, our followers (شيعتنا) are the possessors of the four eyes: two eyes in the head and two eyes in the heart. Know that all creatures (الخلائق) are likewise, except that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), has opened your eyes and blinded their eyes.

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, blindness is the blindness of the heart. Then He recited the verse.

(٤٧) وَيَسْتَعْجِلُونَكَ بِالْعَذابِ ** (They ask you to hasten the punishment) the one promised. Al-Qummi says: That was because the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله told them that the punishment had come to them, so they said, "Where is the punishment?" and they sought to hasten it. وَلَنْ يُخْلِفَ اللهُ وَعْدَهُ وَإِنَّ يَوْماً عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ كَأَلْفِ سَنَةٍ مِمَّا تَعُدُّونَ ** (Allah will never break His promise, and indeed a day with your Lord is like a thousand years of what you count.) And it was recited with the yā’ (بالياء).

In Irshad al-Mufid, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When al-Qa’im (peace be upon him) rises, He will travel to Kufa and demolish four mosques therein. He will not leave a mosque on the face of the earth that has prominence (شرف) except that He will demolish it and level it (جّما). He will widen the main road (الطريق الأعظم), break every protruding wing (جناح) into the road, and abolish the latrines (الكنف) and spouts (الميازيب) leading onto the roads. He will not leave any innovation (بدعة) except that He will remove it, nor any tradition (سنّة) except that He will establish it. He will conquer Constantinople (قسطنطينة), China (الصين), and the mountains of Daylam (جبال الدّيلم). He will remain in that state for seven years, the measure of each year being ten years of these years of yours. Then Allah will do what He wills. It was asked: "How will the years be prolonged?" He said: "Allah will command the celestial sphere (الفلك) to slow down (باللّبوث) and reduce its movement (وقلّة الحركة), so the days and years will be prolonged because of that." It was asked: "They say that if the celestial sphere changes, it will be corrupted." He said: "That is the saying of the heretics (الزّنادقة). As for the Muslims, they have no way to claim that. Allah has already split the moon for His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, and before that, He returned the sun for Joshua son of Nun (يوشع بن نون). And He informed of the length of the Day of Resurrection, and that it is كَأَلْفِ سَنَةٍ مِمَّا تَعُدُّونَ. (like a thousand years of what you count.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from them (peace be upon them), He said: Among what Allah admonished Jesus (peace be upon him) with was: "Worship Me for a Day كَأَلْفِ سَنَةٍ مِمَّا تَعُدُّونَ (like a thousand years of what you count), wherein I shall reward the good deed manifold."

(٤٨) وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ أَمْلَيْتُ لَها وَهِيَ ظالِمَةٌ ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُها وَإِلَيَّ الْمَصِيرُ (48) How many a town We respited while it was wrongdoing, then We seized it, and toward Me is the destination! And how many people of a town, just as I have respited you, while it was wrongdoing, like you, then I seized it with the punishment, and to My judgment is the return of all. (٤٩) قُلْ يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّما أَنَا لَكُمْ نَذِيرٌ مُبِينٌ (49) Say, 'O mankind! I am only a manifest warner to you.' I clarify for you that with which I warn you. (٥٠) فَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ لَهُمْ مَغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ (50) Thus those who have faith and do righteous deeds, for them is forgiveness and a noble provision. The noble (الكريم) of every kind is that which gathers all its merits. (٥١) وَالَّذِينَ سَعَوْا فِي آياتِنا مُعاجِزِينَ أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ الْجَحِيمِ (51) But those who strive against Our signs, seeking to frustrate [Us]—they shall be the inmates of the Hellfire. by rejection and invalidation, seeking to outstrip, eager for those who strive in them (the signs) with acceptance and verification. [This is derived] from (عاجزه فأعجزه) (he sought to render him incapable, so he rendered him incapable) when he raced him (سابقه) and outstripped him (سبقه), because each of the competitors seeks to render the other incapable (اعجاز) of catching up with him. And it was read (قُرِئَ) as mu'ajjizīn (مُعَجِّزِينَ) with the doubling (بالتشديد). those shall be the inmates of the kindled Fire.

(٥٢) وَما أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ مِنْ رَسُولٍ وَلا نَبِيٍ (52) And We did not send any apostle or prophet before you

In al-Kafi, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them) regarding this verse: They added (ولا محدّث) (nor a muhaddith), with the dāl (الدّال) having a fatḥah (بفتح الدّال). It was said: "This is not our recitation (قراءتنا). So what is the Rasūl (الرسول), the Nabī (النبيّ), and the Muḥaddith (المحدّث)?" He said: "The Rasūl is he to whom the angel appears and speaks to him. The Nabī is he who sees [revelation] in his sleep, and sometimes Nubuwwah (النبوّة) and Risālah (الرسالة) are combined in one person. The Muḥaddith is he who hears the voice but does not see the form." It was asked: "How does he know that what he saw in the sleep is true and that it is from the angel?" He said: "He is granted success (يوفّق) for that until he recognizes it. Allah has indeed sealed the Books with your Book, and sealed the Prophets (الأنبياء) with your Prophet."

And there are other narrations with the same meaning in it [i.e., al-Kafi] and in al-Basā'ir and other works.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sajjād (peace be upon him): "There is a verse in the Quran by which Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) knew his killer, and by which he knew the great matters that he used to inform the people about." Then, after he was asked about it, he said: "By Allah, it is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَما أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ مِنْ رَسُولٍ وَلا نَبِيٍ ** (And We did not send any apostle or prophet before you) and nor a muhaddith (ولا محدّث). And Ali ibn Abi Talib was a muhaddith." In al-Basā'ir, there is what is close to this, and in it [it is narrated]: He was asked who speaks to him (يحدثه). He said: "An angel speaks to him." It was asked: "Is he a Nabī or a Rasūl?" He said: "No, but his example is like the example of the companion of Sulayman, and the example of the companion of Musa, and the example of Dhul-Qarnayn."

I say: The companion of Sulayman intended here is Asaf ibn Barkhiya, and the companion of Musa is Yusha' ibn Nun.

In al-Kafi, in several narrations: The Imams (peace be upon them) were muhaddithīn; they used to hear the voice but not see the angel.

إِلَّا إِذا تَمَنَّى أَلْقَى الشَّيْطانُ فِي أُمْنِيَّتِهِ فَيَنْسَخُ اللهُ ما يُلْقِي (52) but that when he desired, Satan cast [something] into his desire. Then Allah abrogates what Satan casts,

الشَّيْطانُ ثُمَّ يُحْكِمُ اللهُ آياتِهِ وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ. (53) then Allah confirms His signs, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise.

In al-Iḥtijāj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a ḥadīth, part of which preceded in the Introduction: Allah, may His mention be glorified, mentions to His Prophet what his enemy will introduce into his Book after him, by His saying: وَما أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ ** (And We did not send before you)—the verse. Meaning that there is no prophet who desired to depart from the hypocrisy (نفاق) and disobedience (عقوق) he suffered from his people and move from it to the Abode of Residence (دار الإقامة), except that Satan, who manifests his enmity, cast, upon his absence, into the Book revealed to him, condemnation of him, disparagement of him, and slander against him. Then Allah abrogates that from the hearts of the believers, so they do not accept it nor pay attention to it, except for the hearts of the hypocrites (المنافقين) and the ignorant (الجاهلين). And Allah confirms His signs (وَيُحْكِمُ اللهُ آياتِهِ) by protecting His allies (أولياءه) from misguidance (الضّلال), aggression (العدوان), and following the people of disbelief (الكفر) and tyranny (الطغيان), whom Allah was not pleased to make like cattle, until He said:

بَلْ هُمْ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلاً (53) rather they are further astray from the way.

And al-Qummi [said]: As for His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَما أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ مِنْ رَسُولٍ ** (And We did not send any apostle before you)—the verse—the general public (العامّة) narrated that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was in prayer (الصلاة) and recited Sūrat al-Najm in the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام), and Quraysh were listening to his recitation. When He reached this verse: أَفَرَأَيْتُمُ اللَّاتَ وَالْعُزَّى وَمَناةَ الثَّالِثَةَ الْأُخْرى ** (Have you considered al-Lāt and al-’Uzzā, and Manāt, the third one, the other?), Iblīs caused to flow upon his tongue: "For they are the exalted Gharānīq (الغرانيق العُلى), and their intercession is indeed hoped for." So Quraysh rejoiced and prostrated. Among the people was al-Walīd ibn al-Mughīrah al-Makhzūmī, who was an old man, so he took a handful of pebbles and prostrated upon them while sitting. Quraysh said: "Muḥammad has acknowledged the intercession of al-Lāt and al-’Uzzā." He said: Then Jibrīl descended and said to him: "You recited what I did not reveal to you." And He revealed to him: **وَما أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ ** (And We did not send before you)—the verse.

As for the specific group (الخاصّة). It is narrated from Abū ‘Abd Allāh (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله suffered a need (خصاصة), so He came to a man from the Anṣār and asked him: "Do you have any food?" He replied: "Yes, O Messenger of Allah." And he slaughtered a young goat (عناقاً) for Him and roasted it. When he brought it near Him, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله wished that Ali, Fatimah, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (peace be upon them) were with Him. Then Abū Bakr and ‘Umar came, and then Ali came after them. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed concerning this: وَما أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ مِنْ رَسُولٍ وَلا نَبِيٍ ** (And We did not send any apostle or prophet before you) and nor a muhaddith (وَلا مُحَدّثٍ). إِلَّا إِذا تَمَنَّى أَلْقَى الشَّيْطانُ فِي أُمْنِيَّتِهِ ** (but that when he desired, Satan cast [something] into his desire)—meaning Abū Bakr and ‘Umar. فَيَنْسَخُ اللهُ ما يُلْقِي الشَّيْطانُ (Then Allah abrogates what Satan casts)—meaning when Ali (peace be upon him) came after them. **ثُمَّ يُحْكِمُ اللهُ آياتِهِ ** (then Allah confirms His signs) for the people—meaning Allah grants victory to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

(٥٣) لِيَجْعَلَ ما يُلْقِي الشَّيْطانُ فِتْنَةً ** (53) so that He may make what Shaitan casts a trial He said: He meant so-and-so and so-and-so. لِلَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ ** for those in whose hearts is a sickness He said: [It means] doubt (شكّ). وَالْقاسِيَةِ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَإِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ لَفِي شِقاقٍ بَعِيدٍ and those whose hearts are hard. Indeed the wrongdoers are in a long schism.

(٥٤) وَلِيَعْلَمَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ أَنَّهُ الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّكَ ** (54) and so that those who have been given knowledge may know that it is the truth from your Lord that the Quran is the Truth revealed from Allah فَيُؤْمِنُوا بِهِ فَتُخْبِتَ لَهُ قُلُوبُهُمْ ** so that they may believe in it, and their hearts may submit to Him with submission (بالانقياد) and awe (والخشية). وَإِنَّ اللهَ لَهادِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ. and Allah will surely guide those who have faith to a straight path. Al-Qummi: (meaning) to the Straight Imam (الإِمام المستقيم).

(٥٥) وَلا يَزالُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فِي مِرْيَةٍ مِنْهُ. (Those who are faithless will not cease to be in doubt about it) Al-Qummi: Meaning, in doubt concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). حَتَّى تَأْتِيَهُمُ السَّاعَةُ بَغْتَةً أَوْ يَأْتِيَهُمْ عَذابُ يَوْمٍ عَقِيمٍ ** (until the Hour overtakes them suddenly, or there comes to them the punishment of a barren day.) Al-Qummi: The barren (العقيم) is that which has no like among the days.

(٥٦) الْمُلْكُ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلَّهِ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَهُمْ فَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ فِي جَنَّاتِ النَّعِيمِ (56) Sovereignty on that day belongs to Allah. He will judge between them. Then those who have faith and do righteous deeds will be in gardens of bliss. (٥٧) وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا فَأُولئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ مُهِينٌ ** (57) But those who are faithless and deny Our signs—for them there will be a humiliating punishment. al-Qummi said: And they did not believe in the wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) and the Imams (peace be upon them). (٥٨) وَالَّذِينَ هاجَرُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ ثُمَّ قُتِلُوا ** (58) Those who migrate in the way of Allah, and then are slain [This refers to those slain] in al-Jihad (الجهاد). And it was recited with tashdid (بالتشديد). أَوْ ماتُوا لَيَرْزُقَنَّهُمُ اللهُ رِزْقاً حَسَناً وَإِنَّ اللهَ لَهُوَ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ ** or die, Allah will surely provide them with a good provision, and indeed Allah is the best of providers. for He provides without measure (بغير حساب).

(59) لَيُدْخِلَنَّهُمْ مُدْخَلاً يَرْضَوْنَهُ ** (He will surely admit them into an entry that they will approve of) It is Paradise, in what they love. And it was read with the fathah on the mīm (مَدْخَلاً). وَإِنَّ اللهَ لَعَلِيمٌ (and Allah is indeed All-knowing) of their conditions and the conditions of their enemies, حَلِيمٌ** (All-forbearing) Who does not hasten the punishment.

In al-Jawami': It is narrated that they said: "O Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, these who were killed, we have known the good that Allah has given them. And we strive alongside you as they strove, so what is for us if we die alongside you?" So Allah revealed these two verses.

(60) ذلِكَ وَمَنْ عاقَبَ بِمِثْلِ ما عُوقِبَ بِهِ ** (60) That, and whoever retaliates with the like of what he has been made to suffer, and did not exceed in retaliation, ثُمَّ بُغِيَ عَلَيْهِ ** by the return to punishment (i.e., renewed aggression), لَيَنْصُرَنَّهُ اللهُ ** Allah will surely help him. Allah will surely help him without fail. إِنَّ اللهَ لَعَفُوٌّ غَفُورٌ ** for the one who seeks victory. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

Al-Qummi said: He is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله when Quraysh expelled him from Mecca (مكّة) and he fled from them to the cave (الغار) and they sought him to kill him. So Allah punished them on the Day of Badr and killed Utbah, Shaybah, Al-Walid, Abu Jahl, Hanzalah ibn Abi Sufyan, and others. When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله passed away, their blood was sought (in revenge), so Al-Husayn عليه السلام and the Family of Muhammad صلوات الله عليهم were killed out of transgression (بَغْيًا) and enmity (عُدْوَانًا). This is the saying of Yazid, may Allah curse him, when he recited this poetry:

Would that my elders at Badr had witnessed The panic of the Khazraj from the impact of the spears.

They would have cheered and rejoiced with happiness, Then said: O Yazid, may your hand not wither!

I am not from Khindif if I do not take revenge Upon the sons of Ahmad for what they did.

We have killed the people from among their masters, And we have balanced them with Badr, so the scale is even.

And likewise, the elder (الشيخ) commanded me with this, So I followed the elder in what was requested.

And Yazid also said, while turning over the head:

We say, while the head is cast down and we turn it over, O would that our elders who passed away were present!

So that they might measure a measure, if it were to be measured, The days of Badr, the weight would be equal in measure.

Then Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, said: ذلِكَ وَمَنْ عاقَبَ ** (That, and whoever retaliates), meaning the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, بِمِثْلِ ما عُوقِبَ بِهِ (with the like of what he has been made to suffer), meaning when they intended to kill him, ثُمَّ بُغِيَ عَلَيْهِ لَيَنْصُرَنَّهُ ** (then he is oppressed again, Allah will surely help him) through the Riser (القائم) from among His descendants صلى الله عليه وآله.

(٦١) ذلِكَ ** أي ذلك النَّصر (61) That (is) because Allah makes the night pass into the day, and makes the day pass into the night, due to the fact that Allah is capable of turning some matters upon others (تقليب بعض الأمور على بعض) and alternating (المداولة) between opposing things (الأشياء المتعاندة). وَأَنَّ اللهَ سَمِيعٌ بَصِيرٌ ** and because Allah is all-hearing, all-seeing. He hears the saying of the punisher (المعاقب) and the punished (المعاقب), and He sees the actions of both of them, so He does not grant them respite.

(٦٢) ذلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الْحَقُ وَأَنَّ ما يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ هُوَ الْباطِلُ وَأَنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْكَبِيرُ (62) That is because Allah is the Truth, and what they invoke besides Him is falsehood, and because Allah is the All-exalted, the All-great.

ذلِكَ ** (That) refers to the description of perfect power (كمال القدرة) and knowledge (والعلم). بِأَنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الْحَقُ ** (is because Allah is the Truth), meaning the Ever-Constant (الثابت). وَأَنَّ ما يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ ** (and that what they invoke besides Him) as a god (إلهاً)—and it was recited with the tāʾ (بالتّاء)—هُوَ الْباطِلُ وَأَنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْكَبِيرُ (is the falsehood, and that Allah is the All-exalted, the All-great), far removed (عن) from having a partner (شريك). Nothing is higher than Him in status (شأناً) or greater in authority (سلطاناً).

(٦٣) أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللهَ أَنْزَلَ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً ** (63) Have you not regarded that Allah sends down water from the sky? (This is) a rhetorical question for affirmation. فَتُصْبِحُ الْأَرْضُ مُخْضَرَّةً (Then the earth becomes green.) The shift from the past tense form was only made to indicate the persistence of the effect of the rain continuously (زماناً بعد زمان). إِنَّ اللهَ لَطِيفٌ ** (Indeed, Allah is Subtle,) His knowledge reaches everything, whether great or minute. All-aware (خَبِيرٌ) of the manifest and hidden arrangements.

(64) لَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth.) as creation and dominion. وَإِنَّ اللهَ لَهُوَ الْغَنِيُ ** (Indeed Allah is the All-sufficient,) in His essence الْحَمِيدُ ** (the All-laudable.) the One Who deserves praise by virtue of His attributes and actions. (65) أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللهَ سَخَّرَ لَكُمْ ما فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (Have you not regarded that Allah has subjected to you whatever is in the earth,) He made it subservient to you, prepared for your benefits وَالْفُلْكَ تَجْرِي فِي الْبَحْرِ بِأَمْرِهِ وَيُمْسِكُ السَّماءَ أَنْ تَقَعَ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ except by His will. إِنَّ اللهَ بِالنَّاسِ لَرَؤُفٌ رَحِيمٌ (and the ships that sail in the sea by His command, and He holds the sky from falling on the earth, except by His leave? Indeed Allah is most kind and merciful to mankind.) In al-Ikmal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم after mentioning the Twelve Imams by their names, he said: "And whoever denies them, or denies one of them, has denied Me through them." Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, يُمْسِكُ ** السَّماءَ أَنْ تَقَعَ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ ** (holds the sky from falling on the earth, except by His leave), and through them (the Imams), He preserves the earth from shaking with its inhabitants.

(٦٦) وَهُوَ الَّذِي أَحْياكُمْ ** (66) It is He who gave you life, after you were drops of sperm (نُطَف) ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ ** then He makes you die, when your appointed term comes ثُمَّ يُحْيِيكُمْ ** then He will bring you to life. in the Hereafter إِنَّ الْإِنْسانَ لَكَفُورٌ ** Indeed man is ungrateful. meaning: highly ungrateful (لَجُحُود) for the blessings (النِّعَم) despite their manifest nature. (٦٧) لِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ ** (67) For every nation people of a religion جَعَلْنا مَنْسَكاً ** We have appointed a ritual a place of worship (مُتَعَبَّداً), a law (شَرِيعَة), and a school of thought (مَذْهَباً) هُمْ ناسِكُوهُ ** which they observe. they follow it and adhere to it as their religion (يَدِينُونَ بِهِ) فَلا يُنازِعُنَّكَ ** So do not let them dispute with you the rest of the followers of the religions (أَرْبَاب الْمِلَل) فِي الْأَمْرِ ** concerning the matter. concerning the matter of religion. In al-Jawami': Indeed, Budayl ibn Warqa' and others among the disbelievers (كُفَّار) of Khuzā'ah said to the Muslims: "Why do you eat what you kill, but you do not eat what Allah has killed?" meaning the carrion (الْمَيْتَة). So, the following was revealed: وَادْعُ إِلى رَبِّكَ ** And invite to your Lord. to His Oneness (تَوْحِيدِهِ) and His worship (عِبَادَتِهِ) إِنَّكَ لَعَلى هُدىً مُسْتَقِيمٍ ** Indeed you are on a straight guidance. a straight path (طَرِيق) leading to the Truth (الْحَقّ).

(٦٨) وَإِنْ جادَلُوكَ ** (But if they argue with you) The truth has already appeared, and the proof (الحجّة) has become binding. فَقُلِ اللهُ أَعْلَمُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ ** (then say, 'Allah knows best what you do') of the false argumentation (المجادلة الباطلة) and other things, and He will requite you for it. And this is a threat (وعيد) containing gentleness (رفق). (٦٩) اللهُ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَكُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ فِيما كُنْتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ ** (Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection concerning that about which you used to differ.) regarding the matter of religion. (٧٠) أَلَمْ تَعْلَمْ أَنَّ اللهَ يَعْلَمُ ما فِي السَّماءِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (Do you not know that Allah knows whatever is in the heaven and the earth?) so nothing is hidden from Him. إِنَّ ذلِكَ فِي كِتابٍ ** (Indeed, that is in a Book) which is the Tablet (اللوح). He wrote it therein before He created it. إِنَّ ذلِكَ ** (Indeed, that) its affirmation in the Tablet (اللوح) or the judgment between you **عَلَى اللهِ يَسِيرٌ ** (is easy for Allah.)

(٧١) يَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ما لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ بِهِ سُلْطاناً ** (71) They worship besides Allah that for which He has not sent down any authority, A proof (حجّة) indicating the permissibility of worshipping it. وَما لَيْسَ لَهُمْ بِهِ عِلْمٌ وَما لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ نَصِيرٍ ** and that of which they have no knowledge. And the wrongdoers have no helper. (٧٢) وَإِذا تُتْلى عَلَيْهِمْ آياتُنا ** (72) When Our signs are recited to them from the Quran بَيِّناتٍ ** as clear proofs, clear in their indication (الدّلالة) of the true doctrines (العقائد الحقّة) and the Divine rulings (الأحكام الآلهيّة) تَعْرِفُ فِي وُجُوهِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الْمُنْكَرَ ** you recognize denial in the faces of the faithless. The denial (الإنكار) due to the excessiveness of their rejection (نكيرهم) of the truth and their rage (غيظهم) over falsehoods which they adopted through imitation (تقليداً). And this is the utmost degree of ignorance (الجهالة). يَكادُونَ يَسْطُونَ بِالَّذِينَ يَتْلُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ آياتِنا ** They are about to assault those who recite Our signs to them. They leap (يثبون) and strike (يبطشون) them. قُلْ أَفَأُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِشَرٍّ مِنْ ذلِكُمُ ** Say, 'Shall I inform you of something worse than that? Than that (which stems) from your rage (غيظكم) against the reciters and your distress (ضجركم) over what they recited to you. النَّارُ وَعَدَهَا اللهُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ ** The Fire, which Allah has promised the faithless, and it is an evil destination.' The Fire.

(73) يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ ضُرِبَ مَثَلٌ فَاسْتَمِعُوا لَهُ (O mankind! A parable is drawn, so listen to it) listening with contemplation (تدبّر) and reflection (تفكّر). إِنَّ الَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ (Indeed those whom you invoke besides Allah) meaning the idols, they shall not create a fly, they are unable to create it (قلقه) despite its smallness, وَلَوِ اجْتَمَعُوا لَهُ (even if they rally together for it) even if they cooperate in its creation. وَإِنْ يَسْلُبْهُمُ الذُّبابُ شَيْئاً لا يَسْتَنْقِذُوهُ مِنْهُ ضَعُفَ الطَّالِبُ وَالْمَطْلُوبُ (And if the fly should snatch away something from them, they cannot recover it from it. How weak is the seeker and the sought!) So how can they be deities (آلِهة) capable of all things that are possible (المقدورات)?

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Quraysh used to smear the idols that were around the Ka'bah with musk (المسْك) and ambergris (العنبر). Yaghuth was opposite the door, Ya'uq was to the right of the Ka'bah, and Nasr was to its left. When they entered, they would fall down prostrating to Yaghuth without carving (ولا ينحتون), then they would turn facing Ya'uq, then they would turn to the left facing Nasr. Then they would perform the talbiyah (يلبّون), saying: "Here I am, O Allah, here I am. Here I am, You have no partner, except a partner who is Yours; You own him and what He owns." He (peace be upon him) said: Then Allah sent a green fly that had four wings, and it did not leave anything of that musk (المسْك) and ambergris (العنبر) except that it ate it. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ ضُرِبَ مَثَلٌ (O mankind! A parable is drawn) [the rest of] the Ayah.

(٧٤) ما قَدَرُوا اللهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِ ** (They did not measure Allah with His true measure) They did not know Him with the true knowledge of Him, since they associated partners with Him and named with His Name that which is the furthest of things from Him in suitability (مناسبة). And a narrative (حديث) concerning it has already passed in Surat al-An'am, and the narrative (الحديث) regarding its exegesis (تفسيره) will come in Surat az-Zumar, if Allah wills. إِنَّ اللهَ لَقَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ ** (Indeed Allah is All-strong, All-mighty) nothing overcomes Him.

(٧٥) اللهُ يَصْطَفِي He chooses مِنَ الْمَلائِكَةِ رُسُلاً envoys who mediate between Him and the prophets (الأنبياء) with revelation (بالوحي). Al-Qummi (said): And they are Jibrāʾīl (جبرئيل), Mīkāʾīl (ميكائيل), Isrāfīl (إسرافيل), and ʿAzrāʾīl (عزرائيل). وَمِنَ النَّاسِ And from mankind, meaning, messengers (رسلاً) who invite the rest of them to the truth (الحقّ) and convey to them what has been revealed to them. Al-Qummi (said): They are the Prophets (الأنبياء) and the Successors (الأوصياء). Among the Prophets (الأنبياء) are Nūḥ (نوح), Ibrāhīm (إبراهيم), Mūsā (موسى), ʿĪsā (عيسى), and Muḥammad (peace be upon him and his progeny, and peace be upon them all). And among these five is Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And among the Successors (الأوصياء) are ʿAlī (عليّ) and the Imams (الأئمّة) (peace be upon them). He said: And in this, there is an interpretation (تأويل) other than this. اللهُ يَصْطَفِي مِنَ الْمَلائِكَةِ رُسُلاً وَمِنَ النَّاسِ إِنَّ اللهَ سَمِيعٌ بَصِيرٌ (75) Allah chooses messengers from the angels and from mankind. Indeed, Allah is All-hearing, All-seeing.

(76) يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ (76) He knows what is before them and what is behind them, He is Knower of what has occurred and what will occur. وَإِلَى اللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ and all matters are returned to Allah. all of them. (77) يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ارْكَعُوا وَاسْجُدُوا وَاعْبُدُوا رَبَّكُمْ (77) O you who have faith! Bow down and prostrate, and worship your Lord, through all that He has obligated you to worship Him with. وَافْعَلُوا الْخَيْرَ and do good, and seek out what is good and most righteous in what you undertake and what you leave, such as supererogatory acts of obedience (نوافل الطّاعات), maintaining kinship ties (صلة الأرحام), and noble character traits (مكارم الأخلاق). لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ so that you may be felicitous.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, obligated faith upon the limbs (جوارح) of the son of Adam, divided it among them, and distributed it within them. And He obligated upon the face prostration to Him, by night and day, during the times of prayer (مواقيت الصلاة). Then He said: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ارْكَعُوا وَاسْجُدُوا O you who have faith! Bow down and prostrate, And this is a comprehensive obligation (فريضة جامعة) upon the face, the two hands, and the two feet.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He made all goodness (الخير) reside in a house, and He made its key asceticism (الزّهد) in the world (الدنيا).

And in al-Jawami', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Indeed, in Surat al-Hajj there are two prostrations (سجدتين); if you do not perform them, then do not recite it.

(٧٨) وَجاهِدُوا فِي اللهِ حَقَّ جِهادِهِ (78) And wage jihad for the sake of Allah, a jihad worthy of Him. (Against) the manifest and the hidden enemies. It is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله that when He returned from the Battle of Tabuk, He said: "We have returned from the lesser Jihad (الجهاد الأصغر) to the greater Jihad (الجهاد الأكبر)," meaning the Jihad against the self (جهاد النفس).

هُوَ اجْتَباكُمْ (He has chosen you) He chose you for His religion and for His support. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: "He meant us, and we are the chosen ones (المجتبون)."

وَما جَعَلَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ مِنْ حَرَجٍ مِلَّةَ أَبِيكُمْ إِبْراهِيمَ (and has not placed any hardship upon you in the religion—the faith of your father Abraham.) He عليه السلام said: "He meant us specifically."

هُوَ سَمَّاكُمُ الْمُسْلِمِينَ (He named you ‘Muslims’) He said: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, named us the Muslims (المسلمين).

مِنْ قَبْلُ (before) He said: In the books that have passed.

وَفِي هذا (and in this [Qur’an]) (meaning) the Quran.

لِيَكُونَ الرَّسُولُ شَهِيداً عَلَيْكُمْ وَتَكُونُوا شُهَداءَ عَلَى النَّاسِ (so that the Apostle may be a witness to you, and that you may be witnesses to mankind.) He said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله is the witness over us regarding what He conveyed to us from Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, and we are the witnesses over mankind on the Day of Resurrection. So, whoever is truthful on the Day of Resurrection, we will confirm him, and whoever lies, we will deny him.

In al-Ikmal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "He meant by that thirteen men specifically, excluding this Ummah." Then He said: "I, and my brother, and eleven of my descendants."

In al-Manaqib, and in a narration: Regarding the Almighty's saying: هُوَ سَمَّاكُمُ الْمُسْلِمِينَ (He named you ‘Muslims’), Abraham and Ishmael invoked it for the Family of Muhammad (prayers of Allah be upon them), for He is one of those from Quraysh who adhered to the Sacred Precinct (الحرم) until the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله came, and then he followed Him and believed in Him. As for His saying: لِيَكُونَ الرَّسُولُ شَهِيداً عَلَيْكُمْ (so that the Apostle may be a witness to you), the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله will be a witness over the Family of Muhammad (prayers of Allah be upon them), and they will be witnesses over mankind.

In Qurb al-Asnad, narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father, from the Prophet (prayers of Allah be upon them), He said: "Among what Allah granted my Ummah and favored them with over the rest of the nations, He granted them three characteristics which He did not grant except to a Prophet. That is because Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, whenever He sent a Prophet, He would say to him: 'Strive diligently in your religion, and there is no hardship (حرج) upon you.' And Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, granted that to my Ummah where He says: وَما جَعَلَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ مِنْ حَرَجٍ (and has not placed any hardship upon you in the religion), meaning: 'no narrowness (ضيق).' He said: 'And whenever He sent a Prophet, He made him a witness over his people. And Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, made my Ummah witnesses over creation, where He says: لِيَكُونَ الرَّسُولُ شَهِيداً عَلَيْكُمْ وَتَكُونُوا شُهَداءَ عَلَى النَّاسِ (so that the Apostle may be a witness to you, and that you may be witnesses to mankind).'" (The rest of the Hadith).

فَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكاةَ (So maintain the prayer and give the zakat) So draw near to Allah through various types of acts of obedience, because He has singled you out with this favor and honor.

وَاعْتَصِمُوا بِاللهِ (and hold fast to Allah.) And trust in Him in all your affairs, and do not seek security (الأمانة) or help (النّصرة) except from Him.

هُوَ مَوْلاكُمْ (He is your Protector,) your Helper (ناصركم) and the Manager of your affairs (متولّى أموركم).

فَنِعْمَ الْمَوْلى وَنِعْمَ النَّصِيرُ (an excellent Protector is He, and an excellent Helper!) He is, since there is none like Him in guardianship (الولاية) and help (النصرة); rather, there is truly no guardian (مولى) and no helper (نصير) besides Him.

In Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, He said: "Whoever recites Surah al-Hajj every three days, a year will not pass until he goes forth to the Sacred House of Allah (بيت الله الحرام). And if he dies during his journey, he will enter Paradise." It was asked: "What if he is an opponent (مخالف)?" He replied: "Some of what he is in will be lightened."

And in al-Majma': The like of it, up to His saying عليه السلام: "he will enter Paradise."

سُورَة المُؤْمِنُونَ (The Believers)

Meccan, its number of verses is one hundred and eighteen verses

بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)
